segments.s657-g593: نباشد is an archaic negative existential/optative form of 'باشد' meaning 'there is not' or 'let there be none'; جز از introduces the exceptionive phrase 'except by'; رای یزدان پاک is a genitive/attributive chain where رای (counsel/plan) is governed by 'از' and modified by یزدان پاک 'the Pure God' (God as an attributive).
segments.s657-g594: ز (poetic 'from') introduces a spatial/range expression; رخشنده خورشید is a modifier + noun ('radiant sun'); تا ... خاک uses تا to mark the other end of the range ('as far as / down to'), with تیره خاک 'dark earth' completing the range.
segments.s657-g595: ز پند آزمودیم is a simple past clause 'we tested/tried advice' (آزمودیم = we tried/tested). وز مهر چند uses an older connective 'وز' (here functioning like 'and/of') followed by مهر چند 'a few acts of kindness' or 'some affection' — چند is an indefinite quantifier 'a few/some'.
segments.s657-g596: نبود is past negative 'there was not' or 'was not'; ایچ (an archaic/formal word) emphasizes negation 'at all/none'; از این پندها ('of these counsels') uses از + ین (orthographic/poetic form of 'این') to mark source/possession; سودمند is the predicate adjective 'useful', so the clause says that none of these counsels were useful.
segments.s657-g597: گر is the conditional 'if'; ایدون is an archaic temporal/quotative particle meaning roughly 'now/if it comes that' or 'if it happens that'; که introduces the subordinate content clause; پیروز گردد سپاه is a relative/finite clause 'that the army becomes victorious' with گردد (subjunctive/present-form) marking the change/state 'becomes'.
segments.s657-g598: مرا را (here split as م + را) marks 'me' as the (affected) object; بردهد is a third-person verb (bardahad) meaning 'will give/bestow' or 'will take' in older usage — here sense 'bestow'; گردش هور و ماه is the subject phrase 'the turning/cycle of light (honor)/sun and moon' that performs the action. The verb precedes its subject for rhetorical/poetic word order.
segments.s657-g599: مریزید is a negative imperative (plural/polite) 'do not spill' (مریزید = 'do not shed'); خون is the direct object 'blood'; از پی خواسته is a prepositional/relational phrase 'in pursuit of/for the sake of a desire' where از پی = 'in pursuit of' and خواسته = 'the wished-for/desired thing'.
segments.s657-g600: که introduces the explanatory/result clause 'for/that'; یابید is a present/subjunctive form 'you (may) find' or a future-oriented 'you will find'; خود here emphasizes 'your own' or 'the thing itself'; گنج آراسته is the noun phrase 'the treasure, adorned/prepared' — the clause promises that you will find the treasure already made ready.
segments.s657-g561: ندانی is the negative 2nd-person form of دانستن ('you do not know'); here it introduces a clause meaning 'you know nothing'. جز following the verb means 'except' and introduces the list that follows; the list items are joined by و in the normal Persian coordination order. This whole segment therefore reads 'you know nothing except A, B, and C.'
segments.s657-g562: چودیدی is an archaic/poetic contraction of چو دیدی ('when you saw'), forming a temporal clause. که introduces the clause reporting what was seen. آمد is simple past 'came'. بپیشت is a contracted, poetic form of به پیشِ تو ('to/ before you') with the enclitic ت for 'you'. نشیب is a noun 'descent/decline' and is the semantic subject of آمد: literally 'a descent came before you.'
segments.s657-g563: ازاندیشه‌ای is a poetic grouping equivalent to از اندیشه‌ای / از اندیشه meaning 'from thought' or 'without thought'; دور is the adjective 'far/removed' following it. و ز تاج و تخت uses the poetic preposition ز (ز = از) 'from' and lists تاج ('crown') and تخت ('throne') joined by و; the whole phrase describes being remote from counsel and also from royal power.
segments.s657-g564: نخواند is a negated form of خواندن here taking the sense 'to call/summon/name' (not 'read'); in literary Persian it can mean 'will not call' or 'did not call' depending on context. تو را places 'you' as the object (تو = 'you', را = object marker). دانشی نیکبخت ('a fortunate/fortunate-knowledge') functions as the subject/agent that does not call you: 'no fortunate knowledge will call you.'
segments.s657-g565: فرستاده آمد is straight past-tense 'a messenger came.' سری پر ز باد is a descriptive predicate placed after the verb: سرِ(ی) 'his head' + پر 'full (of)' + ز 'of' + باد 'wind' — an idiom meaning vain or empty-headed (literally 'head full of wind').
segments.s657-g566: همه پاسخ is the direct object 'all the answers/responses.' پادشا appears as the grammatical subject 'the king.' کرد یاد is the verbal construction where کرد (past of کردن) combines with یاد (a noun 'remembrance') to form 'recalled' or 'caused to remember.' The clause thus reads 'the king recalled all the answers' (or 'he caused the king to recall').
segments.s657-g567: چنین 'thus' sets the manner. تا introduces a temporal limit 'until'; شب تیره = 'the dark night.' بنمود is an archaic past of نمودن 'to show/appear' (he showed/turned), and روی is the noun 'face' (object). The sequence means 'thus he showed his face until the dark night.'
segments.s657-g568: فرستاده آمد again 'a messenger came.' همی is an adverb (here 'still/meanwhile/indeed') emphasizing continuity. زین بدوی is somewhat idiomatic/ambiguous: زین can be read as the noun 'saddle' (زین) and بدوی as 'primitive/original,' yielding 'from this primitive saddle' (i.e. mounted on a rough horse); in poetry such locutions describe the messenger's arrival. Word order is verb-final descriptive after the initial clause.
segments.s657-g649: بسی modifies the verb خواستند as an adverbial intensifier ('much/many'); خواستند is 3rd-person plural past ('they wanted/asked'). از introduces the source/agent (literally 'from' or 'by') and governs یلان ('heroes/warriors' in the plural). زینهار is an admonitory word here functioning as the object/goal of the asking: 'a warning/mercy' (in epic Persian an interjection or noun that marks a plea). Word order is typical Persian: adverb — verb — prepositional phrase — object.
segments.s657-g650: بسی again functions adverbially ('many/much'); کشته شد is a passive periphrasis: کشته (past participle 'killed') + شد (3rd singular of شدن used as auxiliary 'was'); در دم کار is a prepositional temporal phrase where در دم = 'in the moment' and کار = 'action/affair'; زار qualifies that moment as wretched/tragic. Word order places the temporal phrase after the verb phrase, as often in Persian poetry.
segments.s657-g651: چو is a temporal conjunction 'when'; طلخند is a proper name (Talxand) and is the subject. بر + پیل marks location ('upon the elephant'), followed by تنها 'alone'. بماند is 3rd-person singular (historical present/epic past sense) of ماندن 'to remain' — here 'remained (was left)'. The clause thus gives a temporal setting: 'when Talxand remained alone on the elephant.'
segments.s657-g652: گو is the verb 'say/call' used as a reporting or performative verb; او را is the object with the definite object marker را attached (او 'him/her' + را). به آواز is a prepositional phrase 'with a voice/in a voice' (i.e., aloud). چندی is an adverb meaning 'for a while' modifying بخواند. بخواند here is 3rd-person singular (narrative/past) of خواندن in the sense 'to call/summon' — 'he called out to him for a while.'
segments.s657-g653: که here introduces reported speech or a clause of instruction ('that'/'so that' or simply linking the exhortation). رو‌ای is actually رو + ای (imperative 'turn!' plus vocative 'O'); برادر is the vocative 'brother'. به ایوان خویش is a prepositional phrase 'to/ toward your iwan' where خویش + ش form the possessive 'your/ his own' (khwîsh + suffix). Word order: vocative/exhortation followed by the destination phrase.
segments.s657-g654: نگه کن is the common Persian periphrastic imperative 'keep watch' formed from نگه (a noun/gerund 'watch/keeping') + کن (imperative of کردن 'do/make'): 'do watching' → 'watch/guard'. به ایوان و دیوان خویش is the prepositional object: 'over your iwan and your diwan/royal court' (دیوان here means the court/administrative retinue). خویش + ش again supplies the possessive 'your/ his own.'
segments.s657-g655: نیابی is the negative present/future second (or general) form of یافتن 'to find' (ن‑ prefix negates یابی 'you will find' → 'you will not find'). همانا is an emphatic adverb 'truly/indeed'. بسی here quantifies 'many' and زنده تن is a noun phrase 'living bodies/people' (زنده 'alive' + تن 'body/person'). The clause asserts that many living persons will not be found.
segments.s657-g656: از آن is a prepositional phrase 'from/among that' (آن 'that'). تیغزن نامدار انجمن is a noun phrase: تیغزن 'swordsman' (agent noun), نامدار 'renowned' qualifies it, and انجمن 'assembly/company' names the group. Together: 'from that renowned company of swordsmen.'
segments.s657-g553: همانا is an emphatic particle ('verily/indeed') placed at the start for force; زمانت is the noun زمان (time) with the 2nd‑person enclitic -ت 'your'; فراز here is adverbial/locative ('on high, to the summit') placed before the verb; آمدست is an archaic/fused perfect form (آمد + است) meaning 'has come/has arisen'. The clause order is emphatic particle — possessee — locative/adverb — verb.
segments.s657-g554: کت is the head noun (here rare meaning 'book' or 'volume') and functions as subject; اندیشه‌های is a genitive/construct phrase 'thoughts of …' with plural suffix -ها and ezāfe implied; دراز is an adjective 'long' modifying اندیشه‌ها; آمدست again is the archaic perfect 'has come/arrived.' The word order (subject — modifier — verb) is typical poetic compression.
segments.s657-g555: سپاه is the noun 'army/host' (subject); ایستاده is a predicative/adjectival participle 'standing' placed after the noun; چنین is an adverb 'thus/so' modifying the state; بر + دومیل is a locative phrase 'at/on two miles' (بر used poetically for distance). The clause reads as 'The army stood thus at two miles.'
segments.s657-g556: ز is the poetic preposition 'from' (often introduces source or accompaniment); آورد is the past tense 'brought' (3rd sing.), governing the following nouns as its object(s); مردان is 'men' (object), و is the conjunction 'and', پیکار is 'battle' used adjectivally with پیل 'elephant' to mean 'battle‑elephants' (an attributive juxtaposition). The sequence 'ز آورد مردان و پیکار پیل' thus expresses 'he brought men and battle‑elephants.'
segments.s657-g557: The first word is an imperative/verbal command meaning 'array/prepare' (بیارای) addressing the addressee; لشکر is the direct object 'the army'; فراز is locative 'on the heights'; آر (here imperative/infinitive sense 'adorn/arrange') governs جنگ 'for battle' as purpose. Word order is command — object — locative — purpose.
segments.s657-g558: به رزم is a prepositional phrase 'to/for battle'; آمدی is a perfect/preterite form 'you have come' (2nd person or general past sense) indicating arrival for that purpose; چی ست is an interrogative copular phrase 'what is' (چی 'what' + ست 'is'); رای درنگ is a genitive or compound 'counsel/plan of delay' with رای 'opinion/plan' modified by درنگ 'delay'. The whole asks 'you came to fight — what is the counsel about delay?'.
segments.s657-g559: چنان is an adverb 'thus/so' and بینی is the 2nd‑person present/factual 'you see' (archaic form); اکنون 'now' locates the time; ز + من indicates source/agent 'from me' and دستبرد is a noun 'seizure/encroachment/theft' — together 'you now see such a seizure from me.' The clause uses direct statement order (adverb — verb — temporal — source — nominal).
segments.s657-g560: که is the subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing the result/clause; روزت is 'your day/time' (روز with 2nd‑person enclitic -ت); ستاره is 'star(s)' (literal celestial); بباید is a modal/fused form meaning 'one must/it is necessary' (باید with present subjunctive prefix), and شمرد is the infinitive/root 'to count/reckon' used here to indicate what must be done. The clause expresses 'that for your time one must count the stars.'
segments.s657-g1041: ز + [شطرنج طلخند بد آرزوی] sets a causal/relational phrase: ز here means 'because of'/'from'. The noun شطرنج ('chess') is followed by طلخند (a proper/name or epithet 'Talḵand' literally 'bitter‑smile') and the compound بد آرزوی ('bad‑wishing', i.e. 'ill intention') describes Talḵand. Word order is poetic (preposed modifier then name); there is no finite verb in this fragment — it functions as a nominal clause giving the reason or circumstance.
segments.s657-g1042: گوآن here is an archaic/dialectal demonstrative/quotative (≈ 'that' / 'so‑said') introducing the reference to the king; the following شاه is the head noun ('king'), modified by the adjectives آزاده ('noble‑hearted') and نیکخوی ('good‑natured'). There is no explicit verb; the line works as a verbless apposition or comment identifying the king by these qualities.
segments.s657-g1043: همی‌کرد is an older progressive/past auxiliary meaning 'kept on doing' or 'was doing'. مادر is the subject 'the mother'. ببازی is an adverbial phrase (بِ بازی) meaning 'playfully / as if in play' (poetic contraction), and نگاه 'a look/gaze' is the object: together 'the mother kept (making) a playful gaze'.
segments.s657-g1044: پر از خون دل is a fused description: پر از ('full of') + خون دل literally 'blood of the heart' = 'heart full of blood' (a poetical way to say her heart bled / was full of grief). The following از بهر (از + بهر = 'for the sake of / on account of') introduces the beneficiary/cause طلخند شاه ('Talḵand of the king' or 'the king's Talḵand'). The clause as a whole: 'her heart, full of blood, for Talḵand (of the) king.'
segments.s657-g1045: نشسته is a participial/adjectival form 'sitting' (used predicatively: 'he sat' / 'was seated'). شب و روز 'night and day' gives temporal extent. پر + درد وخشم = 'full of pain and (woe/alas)' — پر از X is a regular construction 'full of X', and وخشم is a poetical/archaic particle meaning grief/alas here attached to درد to heighten sorrow.
segments.s657-g1046: ببازی again functions adverbially 'in play' / 'through playing'. شطرنج is the object 'the game of chess'. داده دو چشم literally 'gave two eyes' — an idiomatic/periphrastic use where دادن with a body-part object means 'to lose/ have taken away' (i.e. 'his two eyes were given away' = 'he lost his two eyes'). The overall meaning: 'his two eyes had been given up to/over the play of chess.'
segments.s657-g1047: همه کام و رایش is a coordinated subject 'all (his) desire and his counsel/opinion'. به شطرنج بود uses the dative/locative به + noun plus the copula بود 'was' to mean 'was devoted to / centered on chess' — i.e. his whole will and strategy were toward chess. بود is the past copula 'was'.
segments.s657-g1048: ز طلخند introduces the cause/agentive source 'because of Talḵand'. جانش is 'his life' (جان + possessive suffix -ش). پر از رنج بود repeats the 'full of' construction: 'his life was full of suffering' (رنج = suffering). The clause states the consequence: on account of Talḵand his life was full of sorrow.
segments.s657-g17: This is a sequence of nominal phrases stacked as modifiers: هنرمند (a skillful person) is the head noun, جمهور follows as an attributive 'of the public' (= beloved/admired by the people), and فرهنگ‌جوی is a compound adjective formed from فرهنگ + جوی meaning 'culture-seeking' or 'one who seeks/values culture'. Word order is noun + modifier(s), typical of classical Persian apposition.
segments.s657-g18: سرافراز is an adjectival/predicative descriptor meaning 'lofty/esteemed'. با is the preposition 'with' linking the qualities that follow: دانش ('learning, scholarship') and آبروی ('repute, honor'). The sequence lists attributes joined by و; the whole phrase qualifies the same person introduced in the previous segment.
segments.s657-g19: بدو here is the dative 'to him' (archaic/poetic form), placing the beneficiary before the predicate; شادمان is a predicate/adjective 'rejoicing' or 'glad', and زیردستان اوی is the nominative plural 'his subordinates/subjects' with اوی as the possessive 'his'. The clause therefore means literally 'to him (بدو) [they were] rejoicing (شادمان), his subjects (زیردستان اوی)'.
segments.s657-g20: The correlative چه ... چه ... functions as 'whether ... or ...'. شهری stands for 'townspeople/city-dwellers' used nominally; از در here literally 'from the gate/house' = 'from the court/retinue' and پرستان are 'worshippers/adorers' (i.e., those who honor/serve him). اوی at the end is the possessive 'his', tying these groups to the ruler. The construction lists the two kinds of people who rejoiced.
segments.s657-g21: زنی بود هم is an existential/past presentation 'there was also a woman' (بود marking past existence). گوهرش is an appositive/possessive nominal 'her/his jewel' used to praise or identify the woman as a 'gem' (the possessive -ش ties the noun to someone previously implied). هوشمند is an adjective 'intelligent' describing that same woman.
segments.s657-g22: A string of adjectival attributes joined by و: هنرمند 'skillful' (or artist), با دانش 'with learning' (با + noun), and بی‌گزند 'without harm/unharmed'. This is a coordinated descriptive phrase that continues to qualify the woman introduced in the previous segment.
segments.s657-g23: پسر زاد is a noun-verb cluster meaning 'a son born' (زاد relating to birth/origination). زان 'from that' links back to the previously mentioned king, شاه نیکو 'that good king'. یکی is an indefinite/numeral 'one' emphasizing a single son. Word order places the born-son phrase before its genitive/circumstantial modifiers.
segments.s657-g24: که introduces a relative clause 'who/whom'. پیدا نبود is the negated past-predicate 'was not apparent/visible' (i.e., 'did not resemble/was not like'), and از پدر اندکی literally 'even a little from the father' — together conveying 'who did not resemble his father even slightly.' Note classical Persian often uses پیدا as 'apparent; resembling' in contexts like this.
segments.s657-g433: چنین داد پاسخ که introduces reported speech: چنین (thus) + داد پاسخ (gave an answer) = 'he answered thus'. The conjunction که introduces the content of that answer. او را بگوی is an indirect imperative/subjunctive construction meaning 'tell him' with او the indirect object marked by the object marker را; بگوی here is the subjunctive/jussive form used for an indirect command.
segments.s657-g434: که introduces the reported clause. درجنگ (درِ جنگ) = 'in battle' (a fused prepositional phrase). چندین is a quantifier 'several/many' modifying بهانه 'excuses/pretexts'. مجوی is a prohibitive/subjunctive form meaning 'do not seek' (م‑ prefix marking negation with the subjunctive stem 'جوی').
segments.s657-g435: نخوانم is first‑person negative of خواندن in the sense 'to call (by a name)' so 'I do not call'. The word order places تو را (you, with object marker را) before the subject من for emphasis: 'I will not call you “brother”'. The trailing نه دوست is an elliptic predicate 'I am not (your) friend.'
segments.s657-g436: نه ... نه ... is the correlative negative 'neither ... nor ...'. مغز تو از دوده ما literally 'your core (essence) from our soot' uses از to mark origin ('not born/of our substance'); پوست is 'skin' — the speaker denies any intimate kinship or shared substance.
segments.s657-g437: همه پادشاهی تو = 'your entire kingdom' with همه modifying پادشاهی. ویران کنی uses کنی (subjunctive/optative of کردن) with a second‑person sense 'you will/may cause to be ruined' — classical Persian often uses this form to express a warning or consequence aimed at 'you'.
segments.s657-g438: چوآهنگ here functions as a temporal/conditional phrase 'چو آهنگ' = 'when/if (you) set the intent/plan'; جنگ دلیران = 'war against/on the brave'. کنی repeats the subjunctive/optative 'you do/make' governing the preceding verbal phrase (i.e. 'when you set out to wage war...').
segments.s657-g439: همه بدسگالان is the subject 'all the ill‑omened/ill‑fated ones'. به نزد means 'to the presence of / before (someone)'; تواند is a 3rd‑person singular form 'can / is able' used with a plural subject (classical Persian often uses singular verb forms with collective/plural nouns). The clause states the possibility of bringing those ill‑omened people into someone's presence.
segments.s657-g440: به + روز (به بهرام روز) marks time 'on the day of Bahram' (بهرام here names the martial/auspicious day). اورمزد تواند is a separate clause 'Ahura Mazda can' (اورمزد = the god, تواند = 'is able'), stressing divine ability to accomplish what was just mentioned.
segments.s657-g521: که گویند کز بهر تخت وکلاه is a clause-head introducing reported speech: که + گویند = 'that they say'. کز here is the fused classical form (که + از) linking the reported content and functions like 'that/for' in 'that for the sake of...'. بهر (governing تخت و کلاه) means 'for/the sake of'. Word order is typical older Persian: introducer (که گویند), then the prepositional phrase of purpose.
segments.s657-g522: چرا ساخت طلخند و گو رزمگاه contains the reported predicate. چرا here is the interrogative/expectational 'why' or rhetorical 'that' (here functioning as 'that' introducing the reason/motive), ساخت is past tense 'made/put on'. طلخند is a poetic noun 'wry/bitter smile' and گو is the verb 'to say/call' (classical usage) followed by رزمگاه 'battlefield' as its object: 'made a wry smile and called it a battlefield.' The sequence 'ساخت ... و گو ...' links two coordinated actions.
segments.s657-g523: به گوهر مگر هم نژاده نیند is a short clause offering a conjecture: به گوهر 'in essence/by nature', مگر here functions as a modal 'perhaps' (an archaic/rhetorical use), هم نژاده 'of the same birth/lineage', and نیند is an archaic verbal form meaning 'is not' or otherwise negating the predicate (here used to indicate lack of that nativity). Word order places the adverbial 'به گوهر' up front for emphasis.
segments.s657-g524: همان از گهر پاکزاده نیند repeats and clarifies the condemnation: همان 'that one', از + گهر 'from the jewel' is a poetic metaphor for noble origin, پاکزاده 'pure-born/noble-born', and again نیند negates ('is not'). This is a nominal phrase with a postpositive negating verb, typical in older Persian poetic diction.
segments.s657-g525: ز لشکر‌گر آیی به نزدیک من begins a conditional/conditional-adjacent clause: ز (poetic 'if/when' or 'from') + لشکر‌گر (vocative/title 'commander of the host') + آیی (2nd/3rd‑person present subjunctive/optative 'you come') + به نزدیک من ('near me'). The order places the addressee/title before the verb, a common poetic pattern for address.
segments.s657-g526: درفشان کنی جان تاریک من is the result clause: درفشان here functions as a vocative/epithet (or adjective 'shining'), کنی is the present subjunctive/imperative-form 'you make' or 'you will make', جان تاریک من = 'my dark life/soul' with the possessor postposed (common in Persian). So the sequence reads 'you will make my dark soul shining.'
segments.s657-g527: ز دینار و دیبا و از اسب و گنج is a list of possessions introduced by the poetic preposition ز/از 'of/from' — here functioning like English 'of' or 'out of' to enumerate items to be given: 'dinar and brocade and horses and treasure.' The repeated conjunctions are typical of classical poetic listing.
segments.s657-g528: ببخشم نمانم که مانی به رنج contains the speaker's resolve and purpose: ببخشم 'I will bestow/give away' (first person singular imperfective/future sense), نمانم 'I will not remain/let remain' (negated present-subjunctive form indicating 'will not be left / will not remain'), که introduces the subordinate clause expressing purpose/reason, مانی (here not clearly matched to the provided senses — contextually a verb-form meaning 'you remain' or a colloquial form; the provided sense list gives a proper name, so sense is uncertain), and به رنج 'in/with suffering'. Overall: 'I will give these away; I will not remain such that you endure suffering.'
segments.s657-g513: اگر is the conditional particle 'if'. چند is a quantifier here meaning 'some' and modifies the coordinated nominal phrase تندی و کنداوری (two nouns joined by و 'and'); تندی means 'haste' and کنداوری here appears as the second coordinated noun. The whole phrase sets up the (hypothetical) things that might not occur.
segments.s657-g514: هم is an adverb here with the sense 'even'/'also' emphasising the concession. از گردش چرخ is a prepositional phrase 'from/of the turning of the wheel' (گردش = rotation/circuit, چرخ = wheel/heaven/fate). برنگذری is a negative present/optative form meaning 'do not pass (by)' or 'do not pass over' and completes the conditional clause.
segments.s657-g515: همه is the subject 'all/everyone'. گرد بر گرد ما is a fixed, reduplicated locative phrase meaning 'around/about us' (گرد ... گرد = 'around about'); دشمنست (دشمن + است) is the predicate 'is an enemy / are enemies' yielding the sense 'all around us are enemies.'
segments.s657-g516: جهانی is the noun 'the world' (here the topic). پر از is the adjectival phrase 'full of' that takes مردم 'people' as its complement. ریمنست is a predicative form built on ریمن + ست ('is Riman/…'), completing the clause 'the world is full of Riman people.'
segments.s657-g517: همان is the demonstrative 'that/the same' pointing to a previously known figure. The following nouns form an appositive sequence: شاه 'king', کشمیر 'Kashmir' (place name), وفغفور (a title/epithet here), چین 'China' — together identifying the person(s) referred to.
segments.s657-g518: که introduces a relative clause 'who/which'. تنگست (تنگ + ست) is the predicate 'is narrow / is constrained'. از ایشان indicates the agent/reason 'because of them' or 'by them'; به ما is the dative 'toward us' or 'for us'; بر زمین places the situation 'on the earth/land'. Together this clause says that, because of them, the earth is (felt) too narrow with respect to us.
segments.s657-g519: نکوهیده is a past-participial/adjectival form meaning 'censured' or 'reproached'. باشیم is the first-person-plural present/subjunctive of 'to be' (here expressing the speaker's stance: 'we shall be / we may be'). از این هر دو روی is a prepositional-nominal phrase 'for/from these two faces/sides' meaning 'on both counts' or 'from both perspectives.'
segments.s657-g520: هم adds 'also/too' or 'indeed' to the clause. از introduces the agent or source 'by/from'; نامداران is a plural noun meaning 'renowned people/notables'; پرخاشجوی is an adjective 'quarrelsome/aggressive' modifying نامداران. The phrase names those who will reproach the speaker(s).
segments.s657-g529: هم is an adverb meaning “also/too” that scopes over the following prepositional phrase. از + دست من is a prepositional phrase “from my hand,” where دست is used in the sense of control/power (i.e., ‘by my power’). The nouns کشور, مهر, تاج are a list of objects that will be supplied by the speaker; the verb that governs them is postponed to the next segment.
segments.s657-g530: بیابی is 2nd-person singular present-subjunctive/optative form (used here as a future/you-will-obtain sense) meaning “you will find/obtain.” همان is a demonstrative meaning “the same / that very,” pointing back to a previously mentioned person. یاره = “beloved/companion.” تخت عاج is a noun + modifier: تخت “throne” qualified by عاج “ivory.” The conjunction و links the two items یاره and تخت عاج as things the addressee will receive.
segments.s657-g531: زمهر here functions as a personal name (subject); it is followed by برادر تو with را marking the (definite/ topical) object/possessive relation to the addressee. The predicate ننگ نیست literally ‘shame is not’ = ‘is no disgrace.’ Word order presents the name, then the possessive phrase, then the negated predicate.
segments.s657-g532: مگر introduces an exception or contrast here best rendered ‘unless’ / ‘except that.’ آرزویت is the possessed noun ‘your desire/longing.’ جز از جنگ نیست is an idiomatic negative construction meaning ‘is nothing but war’ (literally ‘is not other than from war’), so the whole clause reads ‘unless your desire is nothing except war.’
segments.s657-g533: اگر is the conditional ‘if.’ پند من = ‘my advice’ (object of heeding). سر به سر is an idiomatic adverb meaning ‘completely / at all’ and modifies نشنوی. نشنوی is 2nd-person singular present-subjunctive/negative of شنیدن, here meaning ‘you do not heed / you do not listen to.’ Together: ‘If you do not heed my advice at all…’.
segments.s657-g534: به فرجام = ‘toward the end / in the end / as an outcome.’ زین بد is an archaic/poetic phrase meaning ‘this ill / this bad (result)’ (زین here is a demonstrative in classical usage). پشیمان شوی is a verb phrase: پشیمان ‘regretful’ + شوی (2nd-person singular subjunctive/optative of شدن) ‘you will become’ — ‘you will be regretful.’
segments.s657-g535: چو here is the simile 'like/as'. فرستاده آمد = 'the messenger came'. باد دمان is a poetic image 'a gust of the hem/skirt' (باد = wind; دمان = hem/skirt) — 'like a gust at the hem' describing the manner of coming.
segments.s657-g536: به نزدیک = 'toward/near'; طلخند is the place/person approached (here treated as the referent 'Talakhund'); تیره modifies طلخند as 'dark/gloomy'; روان is a participial/adjectival use meaning 'moving/flowing' (i.e. 'moving on' or 'making a swift advance'). The whole phrase describes motion toward the dark Talakhund.
segments.s657-g617: بگیریدش is an imperative (2nd plural) form 'seize/capture' with the object enclitic -ش = 'him'. از پشت آن پیل مست is a prepositional phrase: از 'from', پشت 'the back/behind', آن 'that', پیل 'elephant', مست 'drunk/intoxicated' — together 'from behind that drunken elephant'.
segments.s657-g618: به پیش من آرید is a second imperative clause: به پیش (literally 'to the presence of') + من 'me' + آرید (here used as the verb 'bring' — imperative). بسته دو دست is a participial phrase 'with two hands bound' (بسته 'bound/tied' modifying دو دست 'two hands', i.e. both hands). The two imperatives are coordinated (seize ... and bring ...), and بسته دو دست describes the condition in which he is to be brought.
segments.s657-g619: همانگه 'at that moment' sets the temporal frame. خروشیدن is a verbal noun here meaning 'the roaring/crying', followed by کرنای in the genitive ('of the trumpet(s)') — together 'the trumpets' roar/cry'.
segments.s657-g620: برآمد is the main verb 'arose/issued forth'. زدهلیز is the fused prepositional phrase ز + دهلیز 'from the corridor', and پرده‌سرای (پرده‌سرا) names the place 'curtain-hall/theatre'. Grammatically the clause says that the roar issued forth from that corridor.
segments.s657-g621: همه کوه و دریا 'all mountain and sea' = 'mountains and sea alike'. پر آواز is a adjectival phrase 'full of sound' (پر = full, followed by the noun it is full of), and گشت is the past verb 'became' — 'became full of sound.'
segments.s657-g622: توگفتی is the past 'you said' introducing direct speech or reported speech. سپهر روان is a noun + adjective 'the moving/flowing sky/heavens' and بازگشت is the past verb 'returned' agreeing with that subject; the whole clause is what was said ('you said, the heavens returned').
segments.s657-g623: ز بس = 'from/for so much' (here 'because of so great an amount'). نعره و چاک چاک تبر is a coordinated phrase: نعره 'shouting/cries' and چاک چاک تبر (reduplicated چاک) an onomatopoeic description of axes cleaving/splitting — i.e. 'shouts and the axes' rending/clanging.'
segments.s657-g624: ندانست is past negative 'did not know' (ن- negative + دانست). کس here functions as 'no one' (so the phrase means 'no one knew'). پای گیتی ز سر is a compact expression 'the foot of the world from its head' — 'could not distinguish the world's foot from its head' (i.e. could not tell up from down). ز here is 'from'.
segments.s657-g505: ازین گردش رزم و این کارزار is a poetic noun phrase. ازین is the archaic fused form of از + این (= 'about/from this'); گردش رزم means 'the turning/cycle of battle' (گردش = turn, رزم = battle); و links to the next noun, این کارزار ('this battle / battlefield'). Word order is noun modifiers before the head (گردش رزم = 'turn of battle').
segments.s657-g506: همی‌خواهد is an older/perhaps poetic 3rd-person present form with the prefix همی- marking continuous or habitual aspect ('he will / he is bent to'). از marks the source/recipient ('from / of'), so همی‌خواهد از داور کردگار = 'he will ask of the Judge, the Creator.' داور here is an epithet for God (divine judge) qualified by کردگار.
segments.s657-g507: که introduces a relative clause ('who/that'). گرداند is causative/agentive 'causes to turn / makes (something) turn'. اندر دلت = اندر + دل + -ت (دلت = 'in your heart' with the enclitic -ت = 'your'). هوش ومهر are coordinated nouns 'intellect (awareness) and love' that are the things being caused to turn.
segments.s657-g508: به تابی here expresses the result/state into which those feelings are turned: به + تابی ('to/into a state of تاب' — تاب here poetically denotes a suffering/disturbance/instability). ز is the poetic form of از = 'because of / from', and جنگ برادر توچهر = 'the war of your brother Toochahr' (برادر marks kinship; توچهر is the name).
segments.s657-g509: به here marks the addressee ('to'). فرزانه‌ای is an indefinite/vocative 'a wise man / sage' (the -ی ending is classical/poetic). کو functions as a classical relative 'who/is who' (older usage = 'who is'), and به نزدیک تست literally 'is near you' — تست is an archaic copula (= توست 'you are' or 'is yours') used here in the classical phrase 'کو به نزدیک تست' = 'who is near you.'
segments.s657-g510: فروزنده is a participial noun 'the one who lights/illuminates' (here used as a predicate/title). جان تاریک = 'dark soul' (جان = soul, تاریک = dark/obscure). تست again is the archaic copula (classical توست = 'you are'), so the clause states 'you are the illuminator of the dark soul.'
segments.s657-g511: بپرس is an imperative 'ask!'. از شمار = 'from/of the count' (شمار = number, enumeration, or 'reckoner' in context), so بپرس از شمار ده و دو و هفت = 'ask of the numbers ten, two, and seven' (classical/astrological numbering). The repeated و simply coordinates the three numerals.
segments.s657-g512: که introduces the indirect question/relative clause. چون here means 'how' (interrogative). خواهد is the modal/future-like verb 'will/does wish' used here as future/auxiliary meaning 'will (happen)'. این کار بیداد رفت = 'this affair of injustice will go/turn' — رفت (literal 'went/go') is used with خواهد to form 'will proceed / will be carried out' in poetic usage; word order is slightly inverted for rhythm.
segments.s657-g545: بریده is a past participle placed before the noun (poetic order) meaning “cut off.” زوانت = زوان + ـت is the object “your tongue/speech.” بشمشیر is an instrumental phrase “by/with a sword.” بد is an adjective/adverb qualifying the preceding instrument, “evil/bad.” Together they mean “your tongue has been cut off by an evil sword.”
segments.s657-g546: تنت (تن + ـت) = “your body.” سوخته is a perfect/passive participle “burned/scorched.” ز is the poetic instrumental/preposition “by/from.” آتش = “fire.” هیربد is a title “hirbod,” here “magus/chief priest,” so ز آتش هیربد = “by the magus’s fire.” The clause reads “your body is burned by the magus’s fire.”
segments.s657-g547: شنیدم is simple past first‑person “I heard.” همه modifies the following noun group “all, every.” خام here is evaluative “naive/crude,” describing گفتار = “speech(s).” تو is the second‑person possessive “your.” The whole segment: “I heard all your naive talk.”
segments.s657-g548: نبینم is present tense/negative from دیدن “I do not see” (classical usage). جزا here means “recompense/retribution” (what is seen). ز is the prepositional “from/of.” چاره بازار تو is a genitive chain “the market/marketplace of your expedients/devices” (چاره used as “expedient/means,” بازار as “market”). The clause says “I see no recompense except from your market of devices.”
segments.s657-g549: چگونه = “how” introducing the question. دهی is second‑person present/subjunctive of دادن “you give / can you give.” گنج و شاهی are the direct objects “treasure and kingship.” بمن = به + من (contracted) = “to me.” The segment asks “How can you give treasure and kingship to me?”
segments.s657-g550: توخود is an emphatic “you yourself.” کیستی is the archaic/polite interrogative “who are you?” زین here functions as a contracted classical prepositional phrase equivalent to از این = “from this” (i.e. “of/from this”), and بزرگ انجمن = “great assembly.” The whole line asks “Who are you yourself, from this great assembly?” (Note: the provided dictionary senses for زین in the word list are literal saddle/gear; in this syntactic position زین = از + این ‘from this’ and does not match those listed senses.)
segments.s657-g551: The list توانایی و گنج و شاهی names three possessions: “ability, treasure, and kingship.” مراست is a classical contraction of مرا است (مرا = “of me” / “mine”, است = “is”), so the predicate means “belong to me / are mine.”
segments.s657-g552: ز خورشید تا indicates a range “from the sun to …” where تا = “as far as / even to.” آب و ماهی = “water and fish” (a poetic catalogue of natural domains). مراست repeats the possessive copula “are mine” (مرا است). The clause says “from the sun even to the water and the fish are mine.”
segments.s657-g161: هم introduces the clause as 'also' or 'too.' از + دشمن + آژیر بودن is a poetic verbal phrase meaning 'to be/sound an alarm against the enemy' (آژیر here = alarm/siren); بودن is the infinitival/copular verb 'to be.' به جنگ is a directional/locative phrase 'in/for battle' that attaches to the whole clause.
segments.s657-g162: نگه‌داشتن is an infinitive 'to keep, to preserve'; بهره is 'share/portion' and governs the following complement (نام و ننگ = 'name and shame'). The phrase means literally 'to preserve the share/portion of name and shame' (poetically: to guard one’s portion of reputation and disgrace).
segments.s657-g163: ز here is poetic for از ('about/from'); the repeated ز introduces the contrasting pair داد و بیداد ('justice and injustice'). شهر و سپاه are the things being asked about (the city and the army) and serve as the semantic domain of that questioning.
segments.s657-g164: بپرسد is a 3rd-person singular verb form (here used predictively: 'will ask'/'asks'); word order is inverted for poetry, so the verb precedes the subject. خداوند خورشید و ماه reads as a compound/title ('the Lord [of] the Sun and Moon' or 'the Lord, Sun and Moon') functioning as the subject that does the asking.
segments.s657-g165: اگر introduces a conditional 'if.' پشه is a small/insignificant creature ('gnat'/'mosquito' used proverbially). از شاه یابد ستم is an object construction where یابد ('gets/receives') takes ستم ('injustice/oppression') as its object — 'if a gnat receives injustice from the king.'
segments.s657-g166: روانش is روان + enclitic -ش 'his/its soul/spirit.' به دوزخ بماند uses به as directional 'into/to' and بماند (3rd-person subjunctive/present) expresses continuation/result 'will remain' or 'shall remain.' دژم is an adjective placed after the verb meaning 'sullen/grim,' describing the state of that soul in hell.
segments.s657-g167: This is a comparative construction: جهان (the world) + از (than) + شب تیره (a dark night), followed by تاریک‌تر the comparative 'darker.' The double adjectives تیره تاریک‌تر intensify the darkness poetically ('darker than a dark night').
segments.s657-g168: دلی is a substantive form meaning 'a heart' or 'a disposition.' باید is impersonal/modal 'is needed/one must.' ازموی is a fused poetic form meaning 'than a hair' (از + موی) and باریک‌تر is the comparative 'thinner/finer'; together: 'a heart is needed that is thinner than a hair.'
segments.s657-g81: همان به که is an idiomatic concessive/optative phrase meaning 'it is better that' or 'better that...'; همان here marks the evaluative 'better/just so'. این زن بود شهریار is a simple nominative clause in past tense (بود = 'was'), with the noun شهریار ('ruler/king') as predicate of این زن ('this woman'). Word order is subject + predicate (subject first, predicate after بود).
segments.s657-g82: که begins a subordinate clause giving the consequence or further specification: 'that'. او ماند is a past-tense verb phrase (ماند = 'remained/was left'). زین here functions as an archaic contraction of زِ این ('from these' / 'of these'), linking to مهتران ('the great/elder/chief ones'); یادگار is the predicative noun 'a memento' or 'memorial'. The clause means 'that she remained a memorial of these great ones.'
segments.s657-g83: زگفتار is a prepositional/compound form meaning 'by (his) speech' or 'through speech' (ز + گفتار). او is the agent/subject 'he'. رام گشت is a compound verb meaning 'became tame/was appeased' (گشت = 'became'), and انجمن ('assembly') is the grammatical patient/topic: 'the assembly became appeased by his words.' Note the past/resultative sense carried by گشت.
segments.s657-g84: فرستاده شد is a passive-periphrastic construction: فرستاده ('sent') + شد (auxiliary) = 'was sent'. نزد means 'to/into the presence of' and governs the following determiner phrase آن پاک تن ('that pure person/man'), where پاک modifies تن ('person'). The whole segment reads 'a messenger was sent to that pure man.'
segments.s657-g85: که here introduces reported speech or an explanatory clause ('that' / 'saying that'). تخت دو فرزند را خود بگیر is an imperative/instruction: تخت ('throne') is the object, دو فرزند را ('the two sons' marked by را) indicates whose throne or for whom, and خود بگیر is a reflexive/ emphatic imperative 'take it yourself' (خود emphasizes that the addressee should do it personally). Word order places object before the verb, as is common in Persian.
segments.s657-g86: فزاینده کاریست is a nominal predicate: فزاینده ('increasing' or 'augmenting') modifies کاریست ('it is a matter/this is a thing'), so 'this is an increasing matter/this will lead to increase.' این ناگزیر follows as a noun phrase ('this (is) unavoidable'), with ناگزیر functioning predicatively/adjectivally. The tone is hortatory/legal—stating that further expansion is inevitable.
segments.s657-g87: چوفرزند is a conditional/circumstantial expression 'if/when (as) a son' or 'should a son become...'; گردد is the subjunctive/present-future of 'become' (here modal: 'becomes/would become'), سزاوار is a predicative adjective 'worthy/deserving', and گاه ('occasion/time') marks temporal circumstance. Together the segment means 'if/when a son becomes deserving at the right time.'
segments.s657-g88: بدو ده is an imperative or counsel 'give to him' (به + او contracted), where بدو = 'to him'. بزرگی و گنج و سپاه are coordinated direct objects: 'greatness (honor), treasure, and an army.' The sequence lists what should be granted to the worthy son.
segments.s657-g441: گنهکار is a predicative noun meaning 'sinner' placed before the copula; هم adds the sense 'also/too' and modifies the whole clause. پیش + یزدان is a prepositional phrase 'in the presence of / before God'. تویی is the 2nd‑person copular form 'you are' concluding the clause (noun + adverbial phrase + copula order typical in poetry).
segments.s657-g442: که introduces a relative/explanatory clause modifying 'تویی' ('who/for you are ...'). The repeated بد before each noun is emphatic and links three coordinated nominal predicates (نام, گوهر, خویی) connected by و; each bad+noun pair means 'bad in reputation / in essence / in disposition'. Poetic ellipsis omits linking particles (ezāfe) that would occur in prose.
segments.s657-g443: ز خونی is an ablative phrase 'from the blood'; که introduces the relative clause 'that they shed'. ریزند is a 3rd‑person plural present/future form 'they pour/shed' (classical Persian present used for forthcoming action). زین پس is an adverbial phrase 'from now on / henceforth' (زین + پس), and به کین marks motive/purpose 'out of/for vengeance'.
segments.s657-g444: تو باشی (present/subjunctive/optative) expresses a wish or curse 'may you be'; به + noun marks the state ('به نفرین' = 'under a curse'). The coordination و connects the parallel clause من به آفرین 'and I (be) under praise/blessing', with the verb باشی understood to apply to both members (or read as parallel finite forms).
segments.s657-g445: و دیگر که functions as 'and another [thing] that', with که introducing the content of what was said. گفتی is the 2nd‑person past 'you said' (here the input sense list did not include the verb sense). ببخشیم is the 1st‑person plural jussive/subjunctive 'let us give/forgive' (context shows 'give/bestow' on the crown); تاج is the direct object 'the crown'.
segments.s657-g446: هم here means 'also' and attaches to the subsequent list. این مرزبانی is a noun phrase 'this frontier duty/guardianship' (مرزبانی = border‑guarding/charge). و این تخت عاج is a parallel noun phrase 'and this ivory throne' (تخت عاج = throne of ivory). The structure lists further items to be given or bestowed.
segments.s657-g447: هر آنگه که is a temporal/conditional conjunction 'whenever / whenever it is that'. تو is the emphatic subject 'you'. شهریاری is the noun 'kingship / rule' and کنی is the 2nd‑person present/subjunctive 'you do / you exercise' — together 'whenever you exercise kingship'. The subjunctive marks repeated or potential action.
segments.s657-g448: مرا (here split as م + را in the segmentation) is the object 'me' (مرا = me + object marker را). مرز is the object 'a border/frontier' and بخشی is the 2nd‑person subjunctive/imperative 'you grant' (بخشی = 'you give; you grant') — the verb applies directly to مرز. The conjunction و links the second coordinated action یاری کنی 'and (you) give help / aid me', where یاری is 'help' and کنی is the subjunctive 'you do' (i.e. 'help').
segments.s657-g1049: همیشه (always) is an adverb of time placed at the start to mark habitual action. همی‌ریخت is an old/imperfective habitual form (همی‑ + می‌ریخت) meaning 'kept falling/was constantly shedding' and takes the direct object that follows. خونین سرشک is a noun phrase 'bloody teardrops' with خونین (blood‑stained/bloody) as adjective before the noun سرشک (teardrop/small drop).
segments.s657-g1050: بران is a prepositional/elliptical particle here meaning 'for that / upon that'. درد شطرنج is a genitive phrase 'the pain/affliction of chess' with شطرنج (the game) modifying درد (affliction). بودش is the past copula با ضمیر enclitic (بود + ـش) = 'there was / it was for him/it'; پزشک is the predicate noun 'physician'. The clause overall states existence: 'for that chess‑affliction there was a doctor.'
segments.s657-g1051: بدین گونه بد is a formulaic adverbial phrase meaning 'in this bad way' (بدین = 'in this', گونه = 'manner', بد = 'bad/ill'). The lemma list split بدین into ب + دین, but read together as بدین. تا introduces a temporal limit 'until'. چمان و چران are two parallel poetic nouns/adverbs; here they function adverbially to emphasize extent or manner (lit. 'such as' / 'as grazers/shepherds'), a poetic pair used for emphasis rather than literal shepherds. The whole segment sets the continuing, ill state up to a limit.
segments.s657-g1052: چنین 'thus/so' resumes the narrative manner. تا 'until' again marks the temporal boundary. سر آمد is a compound sense: سرآمد (literally 'head‑coming') here functions with آمد to mean 'the end (came)' or 'the time arrived' — an idiomatic use. بروبر زمان is an adverbial phrase meaning 'exactly/upon the time' (بروبر = 'face-to-face/exactly', زمان = 'time'); together they mean 'until the moment arrived precisely.'
segments.s657-g1053: سرآمد here is used nominally/verbally as 'the coming to an end / arrival' and with کنون ('now') forms a temporal framing: 'now has come'. برمن (بر + من) is a light prepositional phrase 'upon me / to me', so the phrase means 'now this story has come to me.' این داستان is the direct object 'this tale/story.' Word order is topical: time (now) + verb/compound + indirect object + direct object.
segments.s657-g1054: چنان هم که ... بشنیدم is a conventional clause meaning 'just as I heard (it)'. چنان هم که = 'in that manner, as (it is) that', where هم is an emphatic/associative particle. که introduces the subordinate clause; بشنیدم is past tense 'I heard / I was told'. ازباستان 'from antiquity' (from ancient times) qualifies the hearing: 'as I heard from ancient times.'
segments.s657-g601: وگر is a conditional particle meaning 'if' (classical/poetic). نامداری functions as a noun 'a famed one' and is followed by the past copula بود 'was', indicating existence in the past. زین here is the archaic fused form of زِ این ('from/among this') linking to سپاه 'host/army' and together mean 'among this host'.
segments.s657-g602: که introduces a relative clause ('who/that') modifying the famed man. The verb افگند is past tense 'threw/ hurled' and follows the object اسب (typical SOV order); تیز here qualifies the motion/steed as 'swift', and برقلبگاه is a prepositional phrase 'toward the heart' (بـ + قلبگاه fused) indicating direction/target.
segments.s657-g603: چو is the temporal 'when' here. طلخند is a proper name in this context (marked by the object marker را), so را marks it as the definite object. یابد is the classical/present form 'finds/encounters' and اندر نبرد is the locative phrase 'in battle'.
segments.s657-g604: نباید is an impersonal modal 'should not/must not' and که introduces the subordinate clause. فشانند is a 3rd-plural present form 'they scatter/throw' (used generically), بر وی is the prepositional phrase 'upon/against him' and گرد placed after the verb means 'around'—together 'they should not scatter/attack around him' (i.e. they must not swarm/encircle him).
segments.s657-g605: نیایش کنان is an adverbial participle (حال) meaning 'praying' or 'while praying' (کنان = doing). پیش is the preposition 'before/in front of' and پیل ژیان is a noun phrase 'the elephant ژیان' or 'the living elephant' where ژیان functions as a modifier or name; the whole phrase sets the scene for the action that follows.
segments.s657-g606: بباید + شدن is an impersonal construction 'one must become' (بباید here is an emphatic/formal form of باید). تنگ بسته is a tight compound: تنگ 'narrow/tight' with بسته 'closed/tightly bound' describing the state one enters, and میان gives the location 'in the middle'—'must be squeezed/tightly shut in the midst.'
segments.s657-g607: خروشی برآمد is a simple past clause 'a cry/clamor arose'. که introduces the content of that cry; فرمان کنیم uses the 1st-person plural subjunctive/optative کنیم 'let us do/let us order' to express an exhortation or proposal.
segments.s657-g608: ز رای is a poetic prepositional phrase 'by/from the counsel' (ز = from/according to), توآرایش is a fused form meaning 'your arrangement/adornment' (here an object or modifier), جان is 'life' and کنیم repeats the 1st-person-plural subjunctive 'let us make/do' — together giving the sense 'by your counsel, let us effect/arrange life' (i.e. revive/restore or adorn life according to your plan).
segments.s657-g449: نخواهم is the first-person negative present/future of خواستن (“I do not/will not want/I will not wish”); که introduces a subordinate clause stating what is not wished. جان is the subject of that subordinate clause meaning “life/soul,” باشد is the present/subjunctive form of بودن used here as a verbal copula “be / remain,” and اندر تنم is a locative phrase “in my body” (اندر = “in/within,” تن + enclitic -م = “my body”). The overall clause means “I do not want that life be in my body.”
segments.s657-g450: وگر is a conditional/concessive conjunction “if / but if.” چشـم is the noun “eye / gaze,” برتاج is a compound prepositional/nominal phrase meaning “upon the crown” (برتاج = on the crown / sovereignty), شاه is the possessor “the king,” and افگنم here is a poetic/archaic verb form meaning “I cast/throw” (compare افکنم). The phrase expresses the condition “if I cast my gaze upon the king’s crown.”
segments.s657-g451: کنون is the adverb “now.” جنگ را is the definite object (را marks the direct object) “the battle/army.” بر + کشیدم here form the verbal compound برکشیدن meaning “to draw up / to deploy,” with کشیدم the first-person simple past “I drew/drew up.” رده (“rank/row”) indicates the formation into which the battle was drawn: “I drew the battle up into ranks.”
segments.s657-g452: هوا شد is a two-word sequence “the air/atmosphere became” (شد = become). چو introduces a simile “as/like,” دیبا is the simile’s vehicle “brocade,” به زر here links the brocade image with gold (“with/by gold”), and آژده is a poetic adjective meaning something like “startled/astonishing” or “striking” describing the brocade-like air — together: “the air became like brocade adorned with gold.”
segments.s657-g453: ز is the poetic preposition “from / because of.” The coordinated list ز تیر و ز ژوپین و نوک سنان enumerates the instruments of combat: تیر (“arrows”), ژوپین (“gambeson,” padded armor), and نوک سنان (“spear-tips” / spearheads). The sequence lists the things that affect the subject named in the next segment.
segments.s657-g454: نداند is the third-person singular negative present of دانستن “he does not know / he does not heed.” کنون places it in the present “now.” گورکیب is a proper name (the horse or warrior). ازعنان literally means “from the reins / out of the reins” and functions adverbially to indicate separation from control by the reins: “now Gorkib does not heed/know the reins.”
segments.s657-g455: برآورد is a verbal/nominative form meaning roughly “raise / bring up” (here part of a causal or narrative action), گه is an adverb “occasionally / at times,” بر + سرافشان describes the manner (“with hair/manes thrown about” — سرافشان = hair‑loose, hair‑streaming), and کنم is the first‑person present/subjunctive of کردن used causatively “I make / I cause.” Together the clause says “at times I raise him so that his mane flies.”
segments.s657-g456: همه لشکرش را is the definite object “his entire army” (همه = all, لشکر = army, -ش = his, را marks the object). خروشان is an adjective/participial descriptor “roaring/tumultuous,” and کنم is “I make/cause.” The clause therefore means “I make his whole army roar / I set his entire host in tumult.”
segments.s657-g569: 'فرود آمدند' is the compound verb فرود آمدن (to alight/descend); آمدند is third-person plural past. 'اندران' (= 'in/therein') locates the action inside that place, and 'رزمگاه' is the noun 'battlefield'. Word order is subject/actor omitted → verb phrase → locative phrase.
segments.s657-g570: The phrase has an indefinite/numeral 'یکی' ('one' / a single item) as object, 'کنده' is a past participle/adjective 'uprooted' (object), and 'کندند' is the main verb 'they did/made' (past, plural). 'پیش سپاه' is a prepositional phrase 'before/in front of the army' modifying where they set or placed that uprooted thing. The verb comes after its object (typical poetic order).
segments.s657-g571: 'طلایه' is the subject 'vanguard'. 'همی‌گشت' is an archaic/progressive past form (همی- prefix + گشتن) meaning 'kept going / was circling'. 'بر گرد دشت' is the locative phrase 'around the plain' (بر + گرد = 'around', دشت = 'plain'). The verb shows continuous action and the prepositional phrase follows the verb.
segments.s657-g572: 'بدین گونه' = 'in this manner/thus' (here split orthographically: ب- prefix + دین/گونه); 'تارامش' is a temporal phrase 'until calm' and 'اندر گذشت' = 'passed on / went by' (گذشت is past of گذشتن). Together this clause says the movement continued 'in this way' up to the time of calm. Note: the tokenization shows ب and دین separately, but together they function as the classical prepositional/adverbial 'بدین'.
segments.s657-g573: 'چوبرزد' is a fused temporal clause (چو = 'when' + برزد = 'he struck'), introducing the time clause. 'سر از برج شیرآفتاب' places the object: 'the top/head (سر) of the tower (برج) of the Sun‑lion (شیرآفتاب)'. Persian often orders the noun (سر) before the 'of'-phrase (از برج ...).
segments.s657-g574: 'زمین شد' = 'the earth became' (شد = become). 'بکردار' is a poetic/archaic circumstantial phrase meaning roughly 'in the guise/appearance of' (here introducing the comparison), and 'دریای آب' is the simile 'a sea of water'. The structure is subject + copula + predicative phrase (simile).
segments.s657-g575: 'یکی' here is an indefinite 'someone' (introducing an unspecified actor). 'چادر آورد' is the verb phrase 'brought a tent' (آورد = brought). 'خورشید زرد' is appositive/simile: 'a yellow sun', likening the tent to a yellow sun; it follows the object as a descriptive noun phrase.
segments.s657-g576: 'بگسترد' is the past action 'he/it spread' (ب- prefix + گستردن). 'برکشور' is a compound prepositional phrase 'over the realm/country' and 'لاژورد' qualifies that realm ('lapis/azure' as an adjective). Together they say the canopy was spread over the lapis‑colored land.
segments.s657-g577: برآمد (past/preterite) is a finite verbal form meaning “arose/issued forth” placed before the verbal-noun خروشیدن, so the clause literally reads “arose the roaring.” خروشیدن is a verbal-noun (masdār) meaning “roaring” and here functions like an object/gerund of برآمد. کرنای is a genitive/possessive or appositive noun (krnā-ye) “the trumpet’s/trumpet-blast” that names what roared; the word order (verb — gerund — noun) is poetic but grammatical in classical Persian.
segments.s657-g578: هم‌آواز is an adjective/adverbial compound meaning “in unison/with the same voice” modifying the following noun phrase. کوس is the noun “war-drum”; از is the preposition “from/out of”; پرده here is a poetic noun literally “curtain/veil” and part of the compound expression از دو پرده سرای (“from the two‑parde/curtained house”); سرای (from the root s̱-rā/سرود#سرا in the lexicon given) functions as a locative/agentive noun meaning a place (here: a hall or house associated with sound), so the whole segment says that the drum sounded in unison from that two‑curtained hall. Word order: modifier — noun — prepositional phrase (source).
segments.s657-g579: درفش (banner/standard) is the subject noun; دو شاه is a genitive construction ‘of two kings’ (دو modifies شاه). نوآمد is a compound/poetic past participial/verb meaning “came newly/appeared” and is placed after the noun it qualifies; به دید is an adverbial phrase literally “into sight” (به + noun دید) — together: “the standard of the two kings newly came into sight.”
segments.s657-g580: سپه = ‘army/host’ is the subject of the second clause; میمنه and میسره here are attributive adjectives placed after سپه: میمنه = ‘right (hand/wing)’ and میسره is an evaluative adjective (poetic/archaic) implying ease/auspiciousness; they describe which host raised the standard. برکشید is the simple past (3rd sing./pl. in epic usage) meaning ‘raised’ or ‘lifted up.’ The clause therefore says the right (and auspicious/easily‑acting) host raised it.
segments.s657-g581: دو شاه is a simple nominal phrase ‘two kings’; سرافراز is an adjective meaning ‘lofty/proud/triumphant’ placed after the noun in typical Persian order for emphasis. در قلبگاه is a locative phrase (‘in the heart/core/center’) with در = ‘in’ and قلبگاه = ‘center,’ so the segment situates the two proud kings in the central place.
segments.s657-g582: دو دستور is another nominal phrase ‘two دستور’; دستور in older epic usage can mean a leader/commander or an order — here it functions as a title/office (two commanders). فرزانه is a postpositive adjective ‘wise/sage’ modifying دستور. درپیش شاه is a prepositional/locative phrase meaning ‘in the presence of/before the king’ (درپیش = before/in sight of, شاه = king). The whole segment lists the two wise commanders standing before the king.
segments.s657-g583: به فرزانه خویش is an indirect‑object phrase: به = ‘to,’ فرزانه = ‘the sage/wise one,’ and خویش (here split into خوی + ش) is the reflexive/possessive ‘his own’ (ش = possessive clitic). فرمود is a past-tense honorific verb ‘he commanded/said,’ and گو is the imperative ‘say/speak.’ Together: ‘He said to his sage, “Speak.”’
segments.s657-g584: که introduces a subordinate clause meaning ‘that’ (reportive). گوید in the manuscript list lacks an appropriate verbal sense in the provided senses (it should be a present subjunctive/optative ‘he should say’ or jussive form), so no listed sense matches exactly here. به آواز is an adverbial phrase ‘with a voice/aloud’ (به + آواز, and به here is the adverbial/‘by/with’ sense), با پیشرو = ‘to/with the vanguard’ (با = ‘with/to,’ پیشرو = ‘vanguard/forerunner’). The whole subordinate clause means roughly ‘that (he) should call out with a voice to the vanguard.’
segments.s657-g121: دل هرد وان is a possessive/identifying phrase meaning 'the hearts of both (those two)'; classical Persian often places the possessed noun (دل) before the specifier. شاد کردی is a past transitive construction 'you made (them) happy' (کردی = 2nd person singular past of کردن) and takes the preceding phrase as its object. به تخت is a prepositional phrase indicating the means/benefit ('with/regarding the throne' or 'by giving a throne').
segments.s657-g122: This is a coordinated series of prepositional phrases introduced by به (repeated for emphasis) and joined by و ('and'). Each item (گنج، سپاه، بنام، بخت) specifies things that caused their gladness. وبنام here functions as a fused conjunction+noun (و + بنام) meaning something like 'and (their) name/renown' and به بخت repeats the preposition before 'fortune'.
segments.s657-g123: رسیدند is past tense, 3rd-person plural 'they arrived/came'; the subject phrase هر دو ('both') follows the verb in this poetic order. به مردی is an indefinite prepositional phrase 'to/at a man,' and به جای is an adverbial/locative 'to the place' or 'in his stead,' marking where they arrived.
segments.s657-g124: بدآموز is a predicate nominal/adjective 'ill‑instructive, corrupting.' شد is the past 'became.' هر دو را is the marked (را) definite object 'the two (of them)' and رهنمای is 'guide' (predicate complement). The construction literally reads 'A corrupting one became guide of the two,' i.e. 'an ill‑instructor became their guide.'
segments.s657-g125: اوفتادند is past plural 'they fell' or 'befell them.' زرِشک here is a pictorial/metaphorical noun placed before the verb (classical poets often use such images); هردو 'both' specifies the participants and به رنج shows the resulting state 'into suffering/hardship.' The clause means they fell into distress.
segments.s657-g126: برآشوفتند is a past plural meaning 'they rushed on/they were roused to action' (a compound poetic verb). ازپی is a prepositional compound meaning 'in pursuit of / after,' and تاج وگنج are the objects 'the crown and the treasure.' The phrase describes them rushing forward in pursuit of sovereignty and wealth.
segments.s657-g127: همه modifies شهرزایشان ('all/the whole of their birth‑city' = their native city as a whole). بدونیم functions predicatively here as 'without/bereft (of them)' and گشت is the past 'became.' Together the clause says the entire city of their birth became deprived/empty (i.e. bereft).
segments.s657-g128: دلِ نیکِ مردان is a genitive chain 'the hearts of the good/virtuous men' (possessed noun دل followed by modifiers). پرازبیم is an adjectival phrase 'full of fear' and گشت is 'became,' so the clause states that the virtuous men's hearts became filled with fear.
segments.s657-g457: بران سان is an adverbial phrase meaning 'in that way / thus' (بران = 'thereupon/in that respect', سان = 'like/as'). سپاه is the noun 'army/host' and stands as the object/topic of the action. اندر here is a preposition 'into / in' introducing the goal. آرم is an archaic verb form meaning 'I array/place' (first‑person singular) used transitively to mean 'I set/array the army'; it is archaic/poetic (note: the provided lemma senses did not include this reading). به جنگ marks purpose/goal 'for battle'.
segments.s657-g458: که introduces a subordinate clause 'so that/that'. سیرآید is a (middle/intransitive) verbal form 'becomes sated / is satisfied' (present/subjunctive nuance 'may be sated'). ازجنگ is a prepositional phrase 'from/by battle'. جنگی is an adjective or epithet 'warlike / of war' modifying پلنگ; together جنگی پلنگ is a figurative epithet 'war‑leopard' (i.e. a fierce warrior likened to a leopard).
segments.s657-g459: بیارند here is a verbal form 'they bring' (the given lemma senses do not match this verbal reading, so sense index is unavailable). گو را is the object 'him' (گو acting as the personal/object pronoun/name + را as the definite object marker). کنون = 'now / presently'. بسته دست = 'with (his) hands bound' where بسته is a participle/adjective 'bound/tied' and دست is literal 'hand(s)'. The phrase describes how 'they bring him' (i.e. now brought, hands tied).
segments.s657-g460: سپاهش is سپاه + enclitic ش (his) = 'his army'. ببینند is third‑person plural form of دیدن 'to see' (here narratively 'they see' or 'they perceive/observe'). هر سو is an adverbial phrase 'on every side' (هر = every, سو = side/toward). شکست is a noun 'defeat/being routed' and is the predicate describing the army's condition. Word order is emphatic: object (سپاهش) then verb (ببینند) then circumstance (هر سو شکست).
segments.s657-g461: که introduces the content/clause explaining the consequence or reason. ازبندگان is 'the subjects/servants of the realm' (here meaning 'the people/subjects'). نیز = 'also / even', putting emphasis on inclusion. با شهریار = 'with the king / alongside the sovereign'. Together this sets up the group 'even the subjects with the king'.
segments.s657-g462: نپوشد is the negative past/present form of پوشیدن 'to put on / wear' (here narrative negative: 'did not/would not put on'). کسی with this negative verb functions as 'no one' (i.e. 'no one put on...'). جوشن کارزار is a compound noun phrase 'jewshan (coat of mail/armor) of battle' = 'battle‑armor', the object of پوشیدن. The clause means that no one among them donned the armor of war.
segments.s657-g463: چو = 'when' introducing a temporal clause. پاسخ شنید is a simple past narrative sequence: پاسخ 'the answer/reply' is the object and شنید 'he heard' is the past verb; آن خردمند مرد is the subject phrase 'that wise man' (آن = that/the; خردمند = wise; مرد = man/hero). Word order here places the verb before the full subject, a common poetic/narrative arrangement.
segments.s657-g464: بیامد is a past tense verb 'he came'. همه یک به یک یاد کرد is a verb phrase 'he remembered/recalled/mentioned them all one by one' — همه 'all/everyone', یک به یک is the idiom 'one by one', یاد کرد literally 'made remembrance' used as 'recounted / called to mind'. The sequence shows he arrived and then individually recalled each person/item.
segments.s657-g345: This is a pair of prohibitions. مکن is the negative imperative 'do not'; ناسزا is its object meaning 'insult/abuse'. The second small clause تخت شاهی مجوی is another negative command: مجوی is the prohibitive/subjunctive form 'do not seek' and تخت شاهی ('the throne of kingship') is its object. The two clauses are simply juxtaposed (elliptical coordination).
segments.s657-g346: مکن again introduces a prohibition 'do not'. روی here functions as 'upon/onto' or 'turn your face/tread upon' (verb-like or prepositional use 'go upon'); کشور 'country/realm' is the noun, and پر از is the idiom 'full of' with از linking to the noun گفت‌وگو 'talks/conversation'. Grammatically the phrase means 'do not go/turn your face toward a country full of talk (contention)'.
segments.s657-g347: چنین 'thus/so' sets the manner; پاسخ آورد is a verb phrase 'he brought/gave an answer' (آورد is the past 'brought/gave'). طلخند is placed after as an instrumental/adverbial noun meaning 'with a bitter/wry smile' and پس 'then/after that' follows to mark sequence. The clause reads 'Thus he answered, with a wry smile.'
segments.s657-g348: به introduces the agent/instrument sense 'by/with' (here 'by means of'); افسون بزرگی is 'great enchantment/charm' (افسون 'charm', بزرگی 'greatness' used as genitive/qualifier). نجستست is an archaic/poetic perfect/passive-form meaning roughly 'was overcome/affected' (the supplied gloss 'was defiled' should be understood here as 'was overcome/struck'); کس is the indefinite subject 'no one/anyone' (context makes it negative: 'no one remained unaffected'). Grammatically, the clause states that by that great charm no one remained untouched.
segments.s657-g349: Subject pronoun من 'I' precedes the object phrase این تاج و تخت 'this crown and throne' (compound direct object). از پدر 'from (my) father' marks the source, and یافتم is the past tense 'I obtained/received'. Word order is canonical S + O + prepositional source + V (poetic flexibility).
segments.s657-g350: ز is the poetic variant of از 'from'; تخمی here means 'from the seed/lineage' (تخم 'seed' used figuratively). که او کشت is a relative clause 'that he sowed/caused to grow' (کشت here carries the cultivation sense), and بریافتم is a past form 'I obtained/received' (بریافتم = به + یافتم, emphatic/compound past). Together: 'from the seed that he sowed I received it.'
segments.s657-g351: This is a coordinated noun list: همه 'all' scopes over پادشاهی 'kingdom', گنج 'treasure' and سپاه 'army'. The list functions as the object/theme of the following clause (what will be held/kept).
segments.s657-g352: ازین پس is the temporal phrase 'from now on' (از + این (written as ین) + پس). به شمشیر is the instrumental phrase 'by the sword' (به = by/with). دارم is the present of داشتن 'I have/hold', and نگاه is the noun 'keeping/guard' used with دارم (poetic order: verb + noun) meaning 'I will hold/guard'. The whole means 'from now on I will hold/guard (them) by the sword.'
segments.s657-g609: وزان is the fused poetic form (و + زان) meaning 'with/from that' or 'and with that'; روی طلخند is an NP in which روی ('face') is qualified by طلخند ('a wry/bitter smile') and comes before the following locative phrase. پیش سپاه is a prepositional/locative phrase 'in the presence of / before the army' placed after the NP, as is common in Persian word order.
segments.s657-g610: چنین گفت is 'thus he said' (چنین = 'thus/so' modifying the verb گفت). Though گفت is the past-tense verb 'said' in usage, the supplied senses list did not include a verbal sense, so that entry is marked accordingly. با پاسبانان is an instrumental/associative phrase 'with the guards'; گاه ('at times') is an adverbial placed after it to indicate occasionality.
segments.s657-g611: گر is the conditional particle 'if'. ایدون functions here idiomatically as 'it comes to pass / if it happens' (a verbal idiom), followed by که introducing the subordinate clause. باشیم is the present-subjunctive/optative first-person-plural 'we be / we should be', and پیروزگر is the predicate noun 'victorious/victor.' The whole chunk means 'if it comes to pass that we are victorious.'
segments.s657-g612: دهد is a third-person present/subjunctive used optatively here ('may ... give' / 'may ... grant'). گردش اختر is a nominal phrase 'the turning / course of the star' used metaphorically for fortune. نیک qualifies اختر as 'auspicious/good', and بر is the directional/prepositional particle 'upon/toward' indicating the intended target (understood: toward us).
segments.s657-g613: همه تیغها is a NP 'all the swords.' کینه را (written here as کینه رابر combined) is the accusative object 'the grudge / for vengeance' (را marks definiteness/object), and کشیم is first-person-plural present/subjunctive/optative 'we draw' (here 'we will draw' as a resolve). The literal structure is 'we draw all swords against/for the grudge.'
segments.s657-g614: به یزدان پناهیم is 'we take refuge in God' (به + divine name; پناهیم = 1pl present/subjunctive 'we seek refuge'). و links the clauses. دم در کشیم is an idiomatic verblike phrase: دم (here 'edge/tip' or figuratively a point/position) + در ('at the gate/door') + کشیم ('we draw/bring'), giving the sense 'we will draw (our blades) at the gate' or 'we will take up position at the gate.' (The verb کشیدن is used here in the sense 'draw/bring'; that verbal sense is not present in the supplied noun-dominated list.)
segments.s657-g615: چو is the temporal/conditional 'when/if.' یابید here is a dependent/imperfect-subjunctive/imperative form meaning 'if you find' or 'when you find' (the supplied sense list flags a grammatical/form usage). گو را is the direct object 'him' (the pronoun/name 'گو' + object marker را), نبایدش is the impersonal/modal 'it must not / you must not' with the clitic -ش referring back to that object, and کشت here functions as the verb 'to kill' in the sense 'do not kill him.'
segments.s657-g616: نه is the negation 'not' (expressing prohibition). با اوسخن is 'with words/speech' (اوسخن = 'speech/words'); نیز is 'also/too' emphasizing that this action is also forbidden; گفتن is the infinitive/gerund 'to speak' used as a noun phrase; درشت is the adjective 'coarse/harsh' modifying گفتن. The line means 'do not kill him, nor speak roughly to him with coarse words.'
segments.s657-g809: بران (’barān’) = 'on/thereon' is a poetic prepositional adverb placed before the noun phrase. زین زرین is a noun + adjective ('saddle golden' in inverted poetic order). بخفت is the past of خفتن ('he lay down/was struck down' — here rendered as 'fell/collapsed') and is followed by و بمرد, where و simply coordinates two past actions ('and') and بمرد is the verb 'died' in past form. The clause reads literally 'on that golden saddle he fell and died.'
segments.s657-g810: همه کشور هند is a noun phrase in which همه qualifies کشور ('the whole/entire realm of India'). گو here is a parenthetical 'they say' (classical usage), and راسپرد is the perfect/past participial compound meaning 'was handed over / was committed.' Together: 'the entire realm of India, they say, was handed over.'
segments.s657-g811: ببیشی نهادست is a nominal-clause: ببیشی ('for excess/greed, literally 'to more') functions as the idea/purpose and نهادست (literary compound of نهاد + است) means 'is placed/set by nature' or 'is established.' مردم دو چشم ('people [possess] two eyes') follows the predicate in inverted poetic word order: 'People's two eyes are set for excess.'
segments.s657-g812: ز کمی بود is a causal temporal clause: ز (= از) 'because of/from' + کمی 'scarcity' + بود ('was'). دل پر از درد وخشم is a predicate: دل 'the heart' + پر از 'full of' + درد 'sorrow/pain' + وخشم (= و خشم) 'and wrath/anger'. Altogether: 'Because of scarcity the heart was full of pain and anger.'
segments.s657-g813: نه ... نه is the correlative negation 'neither ... nor ...'. آن ماند is 'that remained/lasted' (ماند past/perfect sense). ای مرد دانا is a vocative phrase 'O wise man.' The clause ends with نه این 'nor this', so the whole reads 'Neither that remained, O wise man, nor this.'
segments.s657-g814: ز گیتی (ز = 'from/of') introduces the source/domain 'from the world'; همه شادمانی is a noun phrase 'all/whole joy'; گزین is the imperative/optative form of گزیدن 'choose.' The line gives an exhortation: 'Choose all joy from the world.'
segments.s657-g815: اگر چند بفزاید is a conditional protasis: اگر 'if' + چند 'somewhat/a little' + بفزاید (3rd person present/subjunctive of فزودن 'to increase') — 'if it increases a little.' از رنج specifies the cause/means ('from/by suffering/hardship') and گنج is the subject/thing increased ('treasure/wealth'). Word order is literary/poetic with verb before its noun.
segments.s657-g816: همان گنج گیتی is a nominal phrase 'that very treasure of the world' (همان 'that/same' + گنج 'treasure' + گیتی 'world'). نیرزد is a negative stative verb meaning 'is not worth/deserves not', and به رنج ('for/to/at suffering') gives the thing it would be weighed against. Together: 'That worldly treasure is not worth the suffering.'
segments.s657-g361: برآمد is a past/intransitive verb 'arose/issued forth' placed before its noun (verb-first order is common in poetry). خروش is the noun 'clamor/uproar' governed by برآمد. از در is a prepositional phrase 'from the gate(s)'; در here means 'gate/door'. هر دو شاه literally 'each of the two kings' functions idiomatically as 'both kings' (هر = 'each' used with دو to mean 'both').
segments.s657-g362: This is an existential/negative construction: نبود is the past negative 'there was not'. The clitic را after یکی marks/emphasizes 'one/anyone' as the affected element ('not even one'). اندر آن شهر راه = 'a way/road in that city' (اندر = 'in/within', آن = 'that/the', شهر = 'city'). Word order places the negated existential verb after the emphasized object phrase.
segments.s657-g363: نخستین is an adverb/adjectival adverb 'first' setting temporal order. بیاراست is an archaic past form of آراستن ('he/she/it adorned/arrayed'); here it is finite past (3rd sing.). طلخند is a proper name in context (the provided senses list has only lexical senses; see senses mapping). جنگ is the object 'battle/war' being 'arranged/arrayed'.
segments.s657-g364: نبودش is the negative past 'there was not' with the clitic -ش (third-person clitic referring back to the implied delay or hesitation). به جنگ introduces the action 'in/for the fight' (به = 'to/into/toward'), دلیران is the plural 'the brave/valiant ones' (object of implied action), and درنگ is the noun 'delay/hesitation'; the clause states there was no delay in engaging the brave.
segments.s657-g365: سرگنجهای is a compound noun 'chief/treasured riches' (plural) functioning as the direct object/possessed item. پدر follows in the genitive 'of the father'. بر گشاد is a literary/periphrastic verbal sequence: بر as a prepositional/verb‑forming element combined with گشاد (here functioning like 'opened'/'made open'); in poetry such combinations render the past action 'he opened'.
segments.s657-g366: سپه را همه = سپه + را + همه (written together here as راهمه) = 'the whole army' with را marking the definite/affected object and همه 'all/whole'. ترگ و جوشن is a coordinated noun phrase 'rags and mail/armor' (وجوشن = و + جوشن, the conjunction + noun). بداد is an archaic past verb meaning 'gave' (past of دادن); in classical poetry بداد functions as the 3rd-person past 'he gave'.
segments.s657-g367: همه شهر = 'the whole city' (همه here = 'whole/entire') followed by یکسر 'completely/entirely' as an adverb modifying پر از بیم شد. پر از = 'full of' and بیم 'fear'; شد is the past/copulative 'became' — together 'the entire city became filled with fear.'
segments.s657-g368: دل مرد = 'the heart of the man' (or 'heart of the man of wisdom'); بخرد here is adjectival 'wise' (بخرد = 'the wise one') modifying مرد, so 'the heart of the wise man.' بدو = 'toward him/to him', نیم = 'half', and شد = 'became' — a poetic way to say the sage's heart was half turned to/half inclined toward him.
segments.s657-g353: This is an imperative negative clause. ز جمهور وز مای = 'of/about the public and of us' (ز = a classical preposition 'of/from', here marking the topic), followed by چندین مگوی where مگوی is the negative imperative 'do not say/tell' and چندین modifies it meaning 'many/much'. وز here is an archaic/poetic fused form (و + ز) meaning 'and of'. Word order places the topics before the verb for emphasis.
segments.s657-g354: A conditional clause introduced by اگر 'if'. آمنی is an interjection/adverbial here (a poetic formula invoking safety/assent), تخت را is the definite object-marked noun 'the throne' (را marks the object), and رزم جوی is a verbo-nominal phrase: رزم 'battle' + جوی 'seeker/search' (جوی = 'seek'/'search' used as present-stem), meaning 'seek the throne by battle'.
segments.s657-g355: سرانشان is the subject 'their chiefs/heads' (سران + -شان), پر از جنگ is a predicate adjective phrase 'filled/full of war' where پر takes از + noun, and باز آمدند is the past plural verb 'they returned/came back' (باز = back/again + آمدند = they came). Adjective phrase precedes the verb in this poetic order.
