segments.s657-g356: به شهر اندرون is a prepositional phrase 'into the inner city' (به + شهر + اندرون 'inside/within'), رزمساز آمدند is the verb phrase 'they came as warriors' where رزمساز (a compound) functions as a predicative/agent noun 'war‑maker/warrior' and آمدند is the past plural 'they came'.
segments.s657-g357: سپاهی وشهری is an older orthographic fusion meaning 'سپاهی و شهری' ('soldiers and townspeople'), همه 'all' emphasizes totality and modifies جنگجوی 'warriors' (apposition). The line groups both military and civilian inhabitants as fighters.
segments.s657-g358: بدرگاه شاهان is a compound prepositional/locative phrase meaning 'to/at the presence of the kings' (بـِ + درگاه = 'to the court'), نهادند is the past plural of نهادن 'to set/lay/place' used here as the verb of motion/attention, and روی (lit. 'face') is the object of the idiom روی نهادن 'to turn one's face/toward' — so together: 'they turned toward the kings' court.'
segments.s657-g359: گروهی 'a group/some' is the subject, به طلخند is an adverbial phrase 'with a wry/sardonic smile' (به + طلخند), کردند is the past plural 'they did/made', and رای here means 'opinion/plan/counsel' — the verb + رای = 'they gave counsel/decided.'
segments.s657-g360: This clause reports an alternate utterance. دگر را 'another/the other' (marked with را for emphasis/object), بگو is an aorist/imperative infinitive 'say/tell' (here reported speech: 'said'), بود is the past copula 'was', and دل رهنمای is a nominal phrase 'the heart (as) guide' — together giving the sense 'another said: “Let the heart be (our) guide.”' The word order is inverted for poetic effect (verb forms and copula follow the object).
segments.s657-g769: سپه را همه سوی دریا کشید is a past-tense clause where سپه را (army + definite/object marker را) is the direct object placed before the verb for emphasis. همه سوی دریا is an adverbial phrase 'all/totally toward the sea' (همه modifying سوی), and کشید is the past of کشیدن 'to draw/move' used here to mean 'moved/drew (the army) toward'.
segments.s657-g770: وزان پس سپاه گوآمد پدید begins with وزان 'and from that/thereupon' (a connective), پس 'after/then' adds temporal sequence, سپاه is the subject 'the host/army', and the somewhat archaic گوآمد پدید is a compound verbal phrase: گوآمد (an older verbal form 'came') plus پدید 'appeared/emerged', together meaning 'came forth/appeared'.
segments.s657-g771: برابر فرود آمدند آن دو شاه: برابر here means 'opposite/against' and combines with فرود آمدند (a past compound 'descended/alighted') to mean 'came down opposite (each other)'. The subject آن دو شاه 'those two kings' follows the verb in the poetic word order but is the logical subject.
segments.s657-g772: که بودند با یکدگر کینه خواه is a relative clause modifying 'those two kings': که بودند 'who were', با یکدگر 'with one another', and the adjectival compound کینه‌خواه 'bearing hatred/hostile' (کینه = hatred; خواه here forming the compound meaning 'seeking/holding') describes their mutual enmity.
segments.s657-g773: بگرد اندرون کنده‌ای ساختند is a past clause where ساختند 'they made/built' is the main verb; اندرون 'inside' marks location. کنده‌ای is a noun derived from کندن (a dug place/pit) functioning as the object 'a dug-out/pit'. بگرد is archaic/poetic here and functions adverbially to indicate 'around/thereabouts' (its exact lexical sense in this list doesn't match perfectly).
segments.s657-g774: چوشد ژرف آب اندر انداختند begins with the archaic temporal particle چوشد (from چو شد) meaning 'when/when it became'. ژرف آب 'the deep water' is the noun phrase, اندر is the locative/prepositional particle 'into/in', and انداختند is the past of انداختن 'they threw/cast' — together 'when the deep water came/was there, they cast it in' (or 'they cast into the deep water').
segments.s657-g775: دو لشکر برابر کشیدند صف: دو لشکر 'two hosts/armies' is the subject, برابر here means 'opposite/face-to-face', and کشیدند صف literally 'drew a line/formed a battle line' — a standard formula for 'drew up their ranks opposite each other.' Word order places the verb before the final noun صف for poetic effect.
segments.s657-g776: سواران همه بر لب آورده کف: سواران همه 'the horsemen, all' is the topical noun phrase; بر لب آورده is a participial construction literally 'having brought (something) to the lip' (آورده = 'brought'), and کف 'foam' is the object — together: 'all the riders had foam at their lips' (i.e. the horses were foaming).
segments.s657-g497: تو‌گر is an archaic conditional 'if you' (تو + گر). What follows are two coordinated predicates/epithets joined by و; in older Persian it is common to place such nominals after the conditional particle without an explicit copula. نیک‌اختری here functions as a proper epithet/name.
segments.s657-g498: به introduces the target/domain ('for, in') and governs کار ('affair, task'). سپهری is an adjectival/attributive element attached to کار (here describing the kind of affair — military/sepāh-related in older usage), and تواناتری is the comparative adjective 'more capable' formed by the comparative ending (-تری) and placed as the predicate; the copula 'are' is omitted, as is normal in Persian.
segments.s657-g499: ز is the poetical preposition 'from'. فرزانه is the agent/noun 'the wise man'; بشنید is the past tense verb 'heard' and appears before the subject شاه due to poetic word order; این سخن is the object 'this speech'. The sequence is a common poetic inversion (verb before subject).
segments.s657-g500: دگر باره is an adverbial phrase 'again, once more' (دگر = again, باره = occasion). رای is a noun 'counsel/plan' and نوافگند is an archaic compound past verb (نو + افگند) meaning 'cast/laid out anew, devised again'. بن is a short lexical item placed after the verb here; in poetry it can carry a concrete sense ('base, foundation') or act as a terse particle — the sense here is that of a poetic, compact expression attached to the action.
segments.s657-g501: ز introduces the cause 'from/because of'. درد here denotes emotional sorrow ('grief'). The phrase پر از آب روی is a compact poetic order meaning 'face full of water' (i.e., face full of tears): پر = 'full (of)', از = 'of', آب = 'water/tears', روی = 'face'. Word order is compressed and inverted as is typical in verse.
segments.s657-g502: گزین کرد is a two-part past expression: گزین (choose/select) plus کرد as the past auxiliary 'did/caused', yielding 'he chose'. نیک اختری is the proper epithet/name (here split into two words), and چرب‌گوی is a postpositive epithet 'smooth‑tongued / flattering' describing that person. In Persian epithets often follow the name.
segments.s657-g503: بدوگفت is a fused reported‑speech verb 'he said to him'. گو is the verb 'say/speak' used here as an imperative/reporting verb. پیش 'before, in the presence of' marks the spatial/relational frame, طلخند is the compound noun 'bitter laugh / wry smile', and شو is the imperative 'become / be' — together making an imperative request: 'be before him with a bitter smile.'
segments.s657-g504: بگویش is an imperative 'tell him' (بگو + ـیش). که introduces the content clause 'that ...'. پر درد و رنجست is a nominal predicate meaning 'is full of pain and sorrow' (پر = full of, درد = pain/grief, رنجست = 'is (a) suffering' — رنجست is an archaic predicative form). The final گو functions as an emphatic/reporting particle 'say' — a reiterated verbal cue in the command.
segments.s657-g9: چنان is an adverb meaning “thus/so” modifying the whole clause. پادشا گشته is a predicate: گشته is the past participle / perfect of شدن used here as “has become” or “became,” so پادشا گشته = “became king.” برهندوان is the old-fashioned prepositional phrase ‘بر + هندوان’ fused into one word meaning “over/upon the Indians,” marking the domain of his rule.
segments.s657-g10: A simple series of descriptive adjectives joined by و (and). There is no copula here; these adjectives (خردمند, بیدار, روشن‌روان) directly qualify the subject (the king). روشن‌روان is a poetic compound meaning a bright/clear‑souled or clear‑minded person.
segments.s657-g11: ورا here is locative/dative (“there/for him” or “in that domain”), and بود is the past copula “was/was located.” کشمیر تا مرز چین literally “Kashmir up to the border of China” uses تا in the sense “as far as,” so the phrase states the extent of the realm.
segments.s657-g12: This clause contains an older idiom for giving someone an epithet. خواندندی is an archaic plural/past form from خواندن used here in the sense ‘called/named (him)’; به introduces the name given. داد آفرین is a compound epithet: داد = “justice,” آفرین here functions as “maker/creator” (i.e., ‘Maker of Justice’). (Note: برو as printed is archaic/contracted and functions like a particle meaning ‘to him’ or ‘about him’; it is not the ordinary verb “go” in this context.)
segments.s657-g13: به مردی جهانی is an agent phrase: به + مردی (“by/for a man”) with جهانی qualifying the man as “worldly/of the world.” گرفته is a past participle meaning “taken/seized”; together گرفته بدست literally ‘taken in hand’ (بدست = ‘in the hand’), so the phrase conveys that something was taken by a worldly man (i.e., taken into his possession).
segments.s657-g14: ورا here is dative/locative (“for him” / “there for him”), سندلی is ‘a chair/seat,’ and بود is the copula ‘was.’ جای نشست is a noun phrase ‘place of sitting’ = ‘a place to sit’; together: “there was for him a chair, a place to sit.” Note the typical Persian order: possessor/indirect object before the verb.
segments.s657-g15: همیدون is a proper name (subject). بدش here is an archaic contracted form of بودش (= ‘he had’/‘there was for him’); ش is the enclitic possessive ‘his.’ The list that follows (تاج و گنج و سپاه) enumerates possessions: ‘crown and treasure and army.’ This is a rapid poetic catalog of what Hamidun possessed.
segments.s657-g16: The line repeats the name for poetic emphasis: همیدون نگین و همیدون کلاه = ‘Hamidun, a gem, and Hamidun, a hat/cap.’ Here the printed وهمیدون is the fused form of و + همیدون (and Hamidun). نگین is ‘gem,’ و(همیدون) resumes the subject, and کلاه is ‘cap/hat,’ another item in the catalog of his regalia.
segments.s657-g49: This is a location/extension phrase: 'بزرگان' (nobles, subject) followed by a range 'کشمیر تا مرز چین' (= 'from Kashmir to the border of China'). Word order is noun then prepositional/adiunct phrase giving the geographic span; 'تا' here marks extent ('as far as').
segments.s657-g50: 'به شاهی بدو خواندند آفرین' is a verbal clause with the finite verb 'خواندند' (they called/proclaimed) at the end. 'به شاهی' functions as the complement ('to kingship' = 'as king'), 'بدو' is the indirect object pronoun 'to him/unto him', and 'آفرین' is an interjection/acclamation accompanying the proclamation.
segments.s657-g51: 'ز دنبر بیامد سرافراز مای' is a verbal clause introduced by the ablative/prepositional 'ز' (from). 'دنبر' = 'rear/tail' gives the source, 'بیامد' is simple past 'came', and 'سرافراز' is an adjectival predicate modifying 'مای' (a proper name here); the name 'مای' is treated as a noun of person and not the common senses listed.
segments.s657-g52: 'به تخت کیان اندر آورد پای' literally 'he brought (آورد) his foot (پای) into/on the throne of the Kians'. 'به تخت کیان' is the locative phrase 'onto the throne of the Kians', 'اندر' reinforces location 'in/within', and 'آورد' is the past tense verb 'brought/placed' (here idiomatically 'set foot on').
segments.s657-g53: 'همان تاج جمهور بر سر نهاد' = 'he placed that crown of the جمهور upon the head'. 'همان' is the demonstrative 'that/the same', 'تاج جمهور' is a genitive construction 'the crown of the public/people', 'بر سر' = 'on the head', and 'نهاد' is the past tense verb 'placed/put'.
segments.s657-g54: This clause contains two coordinated actions. The first lexeme 'بداد' in the text appears in the data as a noun 'oppression', but here it functions as a verbal/periphrastic element meaning 'he gave' (i.e., bestowed gifts); that specific verbal sense is not listed in the provided senses. 'و' links the actions; 'ببخشش' is a causative/infinitival form meaning 'by his granting/generosity' or 'he granted'; 'در اندر گشاد' = 'opened the inner door/gate' with 'در' = door/gate, 'اندر' = inside, and 'گشاد' = opened/spacious.
segments.s657-g55: 'چو' is the temporal conjunction 'when'. 'با سازشد' = 'with the music being made' / 'when the music began' — here سازشد is a poetic perfect/imperfect meaning 'music began/was set'. 'مام' is a nursing/mother figure (here 'wet‑nurse' or nurse), 'گو' in this line is probably a proper/name element not covered by the provided lexical senses (so set to -1), 'را' is the object marker, and 'بخواست' is past 'desired/asked' (he requested/ summoned).
segments.s657-g56: 'بپرورد' is a past/optative form meaning 'he nurtured/raised'. 'و' = 'and'. 'با جان' is an adverbial phrase 'with (his) life' (i.e., dearly/wholeheartedly). 'همی‌داشت' is an imperfective past 'kept/held' (he continued to hold/cherish), and 'راست' here is an adverb/adjective 'true/firm' (emphasizing sincerity). Together they state that he nurtured and cherished him wholeheartedly.
segments.s657-g169: که introduces a relative clause ('who/that'). از بد is a prepositional phrase 'from/away from evil' placed before the verb. کند is the 3rd-person singular verbal form 'does/makes' (classical/present form) and combines with رها (from رها کردن) to mean 'frees/releases'; جان و تن را is the definite object (را marks the object) and is placed adjacent to رها by poetic word-order.
segments.s657-g170: بداند is the 3rd-person singular form of دانستن 'to know' (here 'he knows' / 'he is aware'). که is the complementizer 'that' introducing the content clause. کژی means moral 'wrongness/crookedness'. نیارد here cannot be matched to the provided senses (the intended meaning is 'does not bring' / 'brings no'), so use -1 for that entry. بها is 'worth/value' ('no value' in this context).
segments.s657-g171: چو is a temporal conjunction 'when'. The phrase is poetically inverted: بر سرنهد تاج literally 'places the crown on the head' but here functions with بر تخت داد as a compound meaning 'sets the crown upon the throne of داد (justice)'; داد is a noun 'justice' modifying تخت (i.e. 'the throne of justice'). Note the classical/poetic word order and repetition of the preposition بر.
segments.s657-g172: جهانی here refers to 'the whole world' (poetic adjective/noun). ازان = 'from that/therefrom' points back to 'داد' (justice). داد is the noun 'justice' (not the verb 'gave'). باشند is the 3rd-person plural subjunctive/optative 'may they be' or 'they will be' in a poetic sense, and شاد is the predicate adjective 'happy' — 'the world will be glad because of that justice.'
segments.s657-g173: سرانجام is an adverb 'in the end/ultimately'. بستر is the subject 'the bed'. ز + خشتست is the poetic compression of ز خشت است — 'is of brick' (خشتست = 'is brick'); وخاک appends 'and (of) earth/dust' (elliptical copula omitted). The line uses condensed classical forms where the copula is fused into the noun.
segments.s657-g174: وگر is the conditional 'if / but if'. سوخته is a past participle/adjective 'burned'. گردد is the 3rd-person singular subjunctive/present form of گردیدن 'become' (classical usage 'may become' / 'becomes'). اندر introduces the locative 'into/within' and مغاک is 'abyss' — together 'if it be burned, it becomes cast into the abyss.'
segments.s657-g175: ازین دودمان = 'from this dynasty/lineage' (از + این contracted). The element ین is the orthographic/poetic part of این. شاه جمهور is a compound 'king of the people' or 'the people's king' (جمهور = public). بود is the past copula 'was'.
segments.s657-g176: که introduces a relative clause 'who/that'. رایش here means 'his rule/reign'. ز کردار بد دور بود is a classical construction: ز = 'from/of', کردار = 'conduct/deeds', بد = 'bad', دور بود = 'was distant/removed' — together 'his rule was far removed from bad conduct' (i.e. he was free from wrongdoing). بود is the past copula 'was'.
segments.s657-g481: بباید is an impersonal modal meaning 'one must' or 'it is necessary'. It governs the following verbal phrases; here the bare verb stems (فرستاد) and the verbal noun construction (دادن پیام) function as the actions required: 'to send' and 'to give/deliver a message'. The coordinating و simply links the two actions.
segments.s657-g482: بگردد is the subjunctive/potential form of گشت here meaning 'become' or 'turn into'. مگر introduces the exception 'unless'. او is the third‑person pronoun; از این جنگ is an ablative/causal phrase 'because of/from this war'; رام is an adjective 'tame/gentle' describing his state. Together: 'unless he becomes tamed by this war.'
segments.s657-g483: بدو ده is an object‑before‑verb order: بدو ('to him') + ده (imperative of دادن, 'give'). همه گنج = 'all the treasure'. نابرده رنج is a compound adjective phrase literally 'not having taken hardship' — idiomatically 'not won by toil' or 'acquired without effort'; it qualifies گنج.
segments.s657-g484: تو is the emphatic subject 'you'. جان برادر here means 'the life of (or life for) a brother' with جان used as 'life'. گزین کن is the imperative 'choose' (گزین + supportive کن). ز گنج is a prepositional phrase 'from/than treasure' = 'instead of treasure' (poetic use of ز). The clause instructs: 'you choose a brother's life rather than treasure.'
segments.s657-g485: چو ... باشد forms a conditional clause: چو 'if/when' + باشد (subjunctive/present of 'to be') introduces the condition. تو را is a dative/possessive construction marking what belongs to you ('to you'). تاج و انگشتری are the possessed items listed ('crown and ring').
segments.s657-g486: به دینار is an adverbial phrase 'for dinars/for money' (be + noun). با او here indicates the relation 'in dealing with him' or 'with respect to him'. مکن داوری is a negated imperative: مکن 'do not' + داوری 'judgment' (object/noun used as the action). Together: 'do not (let) money make you judge him' — i.e., 'do not judge him for money.'
segments.s657-g487: نگه کردم is a compound verb: نگه (gaze/watch) plus کردم (I did) = 'I watched/observed'. از گردش آسمان is a prepositional phrase 'from the rotation/circuit of the sky', giving the source of the observation.
segments.s657-g488: بدین زودی is an adverbial phrase 'with this quickness / thus soon' (poetic formation meaning 'so soon' or 'by this, soon'). او را marks the person affected ('to him'); سرآید is a subjunctive/optative form of 'to come' used here to indicate future/result ('will come'); زمان ('time') is the thing that 'comes' — i.e. 'his time will come soon.'
segments.s657-g417: دل من = 'my heart' (noun + pronoun after the noun for possession). بدین آشتی = بدین (ب + این, poetic 'with/by this') + آشتی 'reconciliation'; شاد کن is an imperative construction 'make (it) glad' where کن is the verb and شاد its predicative adjective. The whole segment is an imperative request: 'Make my heart glad with this reconciliation.'
segments.s657-g418: ز = poetic preposition 'from/of'. فام خرد is a noun phrase 'فامِ خرد' (fām + خرد) meaning 'the hue/trace of wisdom' (an attributive/genitive relation without an explicit ezāfe). گردن آزاد کن is an imperative 'free the neck' (گردن object, آزاد adjective, کن auxiliary 'make/do'): 'set the neck free' — a poetic way to ask for release/relief.
segments.s657-g419: ازین = از + این ('from this'); مرز 'border/limit'. تا پیش = 'up to / as far as before' (تا ... پیش ... marks the extent). دریای چین = 'the Sea of China' (دریای = genitive 'sea of', چین = China). Word order is typical Persian: prepositional start indicating extent, then location phrase.
segments.s657-g420: This segment should be read as the optative construction تو را باد چندانک خواهی زمین — i.e. تو را باد (literally 'may it be to you') expresses a wish, چندانک 'to that extent/as much as', خواهی 'you want/desire' and زمین 'land/ground' is its object: 'may you have as much land as you desire.' (Note: the provided token 'راباد' is here the mis-tokenized sequence 'را باد' carrying the optative meaning.)
segments.s657-g421: همه مهر = 'all (our) love/affection' (همه modifying مهر). با جان = 'with/by life' used for comparison or accompaniment. برابر کنیم is first-person plural 'let us / we make equal' (کنیم is the subjunctive/optative/form of 'to do/make'). The phrase means 'let us make all love equal to life' (i.e. love as dear as life).
segments.s657-g422: تو را marks 'you' as the definite object (تو + object marker را). بر = 'upon/on'. سرخویش here is a fused form meaning 'our (ruddy) head' (poetic compound; کارکرد = locative: 'on our head'); افسر 'helmet' (object predicative). کنیم 'we will make/place' — overall 'we will set/place you as a helmet upon our head' (a proud/honorific image).
segments.s657-g423: ببخشیم is first-person plural (let us / we grant/ bestow) here with the sense 'to give/bestow'. شاهی 'kingship' is the object. به کردار گنج = 'in the form/character of a treasure' (به + noun phrase indicating manner: 'as a treasure-deed'). The clause expresses 'let us grant kingship as if it were a treasure/valuable deed.'
segments.s657-g424: که introduces a causal/explanatory clause 'for/because'. این تخت و افسر = 'this throne and helmet'. نیرزد is the negative-present impersonal/3rd-person form 'is not worth / does not merit', followed by به رنج 'to/for the toil' (به marks the standard/measure: 'worth the hardship'). Together: 'for this throne and helmet are not worth the toil.'
segments.s657-g321: ببودند is an archaic/poetic past plural (they were). زان 'from that' links the clause to the prior news. آگهی here means 'news/notice' and پر هراس is an adjectival phrase 'full of fear' (پر = 'full (of)', هراس = 'fear').
segments.s657-g322: همی‌داشتندی is an archaic continuous/past plural form (همی‑ + داشتند) meaning 'they kept/maintained'. شب و روز is the time expression 'night and day' and پاس is a noun 'guard/sentry', so the whole phrase means 'they kept guard night and day.'
segments.s657-g323: چنان بد که is a correlative clause 'so/that' (چنان ... که = 'so ... that'). روزی here functions as a temporal adverb 'one day' placed before the noun phrase 'دو شاه جوان' ('two young kings').
segments.s657-g324: برفتند is past plural 'they went/set out'. بی‌لشکر is a prefixed construction meaning 'without an army', and پهلوان, coordinated by و, names the missing warrior/champion — so 'without an army or any champions.'
segments.s657-g325: زبان here is used metonymically for 'speech' or 'mouth/utterance' and برگشادند is past plural 'they opened' (they began to speak). یک با دگر is a reciprocal phrase 'one with the other / to one another.'
segments.s657-g326: پرآژنگ روی is a noun phrase: پرآژنگ (ornamented/resplendent) qualifies روی 'face'; پراز جنگ سر uses پراز (a variant of پر از) 'full of' to qualify سر 'head' with جنگ 'battle/fighting' (i.e. 'heads full of war'). The two coordinated phrases describe their appearance and intent.
segments.s657-g327: به here is an instrumental/adverbial 'with' (by means of), طلخند 'bitter smile' describes the manner, and گفت is the speech-act verb 'said' (verb form not well represented in the provided sense list). ای برادر is a vocative 'O brother' and مکن is the negative imperative 'do not.'
segments.s657-g328: کز is a conjunction 'that/for' (introducing the reason). اندازه بگذشت is a verbal phrase 'has gone beyond measure' (اندازه = extent/measure; بگذشت here = 'passed/exceeded'). ما را سخن = 'our speech/words' with را marking the pronoun as the topical/affected element (archaic ordering: pronoun + را + noun).
segments.s657-g985: صفت کرد is a light‑verb construction: the noun صفت (‘description’) combines with کرد (past of کردن) to mean “described / made a description.” The subject فرزانه (the sage/wise one) follows the verb (poetically inverted) and آن رزمگاه is the direct object ‘that battlefield.’ کرد is simple past.
segments.s657-g986: که introduces a subordinate clause (here a temporal/relative sense: ‘when/that’). چون + past رفت functions as a temporal marker ‘when … went/assembled.’ The sequence پیکار جنگ وسپاه lists three coordinated nouns (appositive or synonymous terms: fight, war, and host) which are understood as the thing that ‘went’ (رفت). The verb is past tense.
segments.s657-g987: ز is the poetic form of از (‘from’). The phrase strings three sources with و: ز دریا و از کنده و آبگیر = ‘from the sea, and from stumps, and (from) ponds,’ with کنده used as a noun ‘stump/uprooted trunk’ and آبگیر ‘pond/reservoir.’
segments.s657-g988: یکایک is an adverb ‘one by one / each and every.’ بگفتند is past plural ‘they said/spoke’ (ب‑ prefix is an older/poetic verbal form). با introduces the manner/tool (‘with’), and تیزویر is an obscure compound/name left untranslated in the text; it is the instrument or manner with which they spoke.
segments.s657-g989: نخفتند is negative past plural ‘they did not sleep.’ زایشان is composed of ز (from/because of) + ایشان (‘of them’), here functioning to mark membership/causal relation so that یکی (one) with نخفتند gives the sense ‘not one of them slept.’ تیره شب is an adverbial/temporal phrase ‘that dark night.’ Word order is poetic and elements are juxtaposed for emphasis.
segments.s657-g990: نه is a reinforcing negative ‘not.’ بر یکدگر = ‘to/toward one another’ with بر marking direction/target. برگشادند is past plural ‘they opened’ and لب (lip) completes the Persian idiom لب گشودن ‘to open one’s lips’ = ‘to speak’ — here ‘they did not speak to one another.’
segments.s657-g991: ز میدان ‘from the field’ is a locative prepositional phrase placed frontally for emphasis. چو + برخاست functions temporally ‘when … rose.’ آواز کوس is a genitive/compound noun ‘the sound/cry of the drum’ (آواز governed by کوس). برخاست is past tense ‘rose/was raised.’
segments.s657-g992: جهاندیدگان is a plural agent noun ‘the worldly‑wise / experienced men.’ خواستند is past plural ‘they wanted / desired / asked for.’ آبنوس is the object ‘ebony’ (literal wood); contextually it is what they sought or demanded. Simple past agreement (plural) is used throughout.
segments.s657-g177: نه + نِگاهِ/هنگام phrase negates the following time-clause: 'نه هنگام بد مردن' = 'not an ill/unlucky time to die'. 'مردن' is the infinitive/noun 'to die' governed by 'هنگام'. 'او را بمرد' uses او + را (the object/topic marker used here for emphasis) together with the archaic/past verb form بمرد to state the death (classical Persian often uses forms like بمرد where modern Persian would use مُرد). The clause as a whole means 'it was not an unfortunate time for his dying; he died.'
segments.s657-g178: جهان را is the definite object/topic ('the world/realm'); به here marks the beneficiary/recipient ('to'). کهتر برادر is a descriptive phrase 'the lesser/inferior (often: younger) brother' that serves as the indirect object; سپرد is the past transitive 'entrusted/handed over'. The word order is Subject (implicit he) – object (جهان را) – prepositional beneficiary (به کهتر برادر) – verb (سپرد).
segments.s657-g179: ز + noun is the poetic prepositional phrase 'from/coming out of' (ز دنبر = 'from the rear/lineage'); بیامد is the past 'came'. سرافراز is a predicate adjective placed after the verb (classical order allows postverbal adjectives), and مای here appears as a name or epithet following that adjective. The whole clause reads 'from the rear came the honored/lofty Māy.'
segments.s657-g180: جوان بود is a simple past-description 'he was young' (بود = past 'was'). The sequence و بینا دل و پاک رای strings adjectives/nouns: بینا دل is a compound description 'discerning/clear‑sighted in heart' (بینا modifying دل), and و پاک رای links 'and pure in counsel' (پاک modifies رای). The coordinating و links attributes describing the same subject.
segments.s657-g181: همه is the universal quantifier 'all/everyone'. سندلی is a noun meaning 'seat/seat-holder' (i.e., those who occupy seats of office); پیش (here) = 'in the presence of/before' and is placed before the verb for emphasis. اوآمدند is a fused classical form meaning 'they came (to him)' — subject is plural 'they'. The clause literally: 'All the seat‑holders came before him.'
segments.s657-g182: پر از + noun = 'full of ...' (پر از خون دل = 'full of the blood of the heart' — a poetic way to indicate bleeding hearts/grief). The coordinating و connects that phrase to شاه جو آمدند; شاه جو is a compound likely meaning 'king‑seekers' or 'those who seek the kingship' (جو here is a deverbal/agentive element in classical use, not the grain), and آمدند is the plural past 'they came/arrived'. Word‑order places the descriptive prepositional phrase first, then the parallel noun phrase and finishing verb.
segments.s657-g183: بیامد 'came' is the past verb; به تخت مهی = 'to the (glorious/beautiful) throne' with به marking direction/goal. برنشست is a compound past 'sat upon' (بر + نشست); in classical Persian the prepositional prefix + verb is common. The sequence expresses motion to the throne followed by the action of taking a seat on it.
segments.s657-g184: میان = 'among/between' and تنگ بسته is a juxtaposed adjective phrase 'closely/narrowly closed or tightly packed' describing the surroundings; گشاده دو دست is an inverted adjective‑noun ordering common in poetry where 'گشاده' (open) modifies 'دو دست' (two hands). Grammatically this is an adverbial/visual detail: 'with his two hands open among the tightly packed [crowd/settings].'
segments.s657-g73: سخن رفت is an idiomatic past construction: سخن (noun, 'speech/talk') + رفت (past of رفتن 'to go') = 'talk went/was circulated'. هرگونه ('every kind / of every sort') qualifies the speech; its position after the verb is a poetic/inverted ordering. بر انجمن is a prepositional phrase ('on/at the assembly') showing where the talk circulated.
segments.s657-g74: چنین گفت is a fixed sequence 'thus said' (چنین = 'thus', گفت = past tense verb 'said'); the speaker (فرزانه‌ای رای‌زن) follows the verb here by poetic inversion (normally subject precedes or is adjacent). فرزانه‌ای is an indefinite/animate subject 'a sage' and رای‌زن is an appositive/title 'counselor/adviser' describing that person.
segments.s657-g75: The first که is the relative/subordinating 'that/who' introducing a descriptive clause about این زن ('this woman'). که ... بود ('who was') contains از تخم جمهور بود: از تخم = 'from the seed/stock of' (تخم used in the sense 'origin/lineage'), جمهور = 'the people/public', and بود is the past copula 'was'.
segments.s657-g76: همیشه ز کردار بد دور بود is a predicate about the woman: همیشه ('always'), ز (poetic form of از, 'from'), کردار بد ('bad conduct'), دور بود ('was far/removed'). دور here means 'removed' (i.e. free from), and بود is the past copula, so together 'was always removed from bad conduct' = 'was always free of evil deeds.'
segments.s657-g77: همه راستی خواستی نزد شوی is a conditional/optative expression: همه راستی ('all uprightness / every right'), خواستی is the past-tense/optative of خواستن used like 'if you wished', نزد شوی ('to be near / to be present before you') — شوی is the subjunctive/infinitive-form of شدن/شدن here meaning 'be' (auxiliary sense). The line thus renders an 'if you wanted X to be present' idea.
segments.s657-g78: نبود ایچ تابود جز دادجوی is an emphatic negation: نبود ('there was not'), ایچ (archaic/poetic 'nothing, not at all') and تابود (compound past/existential 'there existed / there was') together strengthen the negation; جز ('except') introduces the sole exception دادجوی ('the plaintiff / justice‑seeker'). The sense: 'there was nothing — there existed none except the plaintiff.'
segments.s657-g79: نژادیست این is a predicative phrase: نژادیست ('of a lineage / of noble stock' — adjective + copular -ست) followed by the demonstrative این ('this one'). ساخته is a past participle/verb 'has made/established' taking داد را ('justice' as its definite object marked by را). Word order is slightly inverted for poetic effect: predicate-marker + demonstrative, then the verb phrase.
segments.s657-g80: همه راستی را و بنیاد را lists the objects established: همه ('all/every'), راستی ('uprightness/right/truth' here meaning moral/right order), followed by object marker را; و ('and') joins بنیاد ('foundation') also with را. The repeated را emphasizes definite objects in a poetic accumulative list.
segments.s657-g873: به کردار + noun phrase is a simile/locution meaning “in the manner of / like.” Poetic word order places this phrase before the noun it describes: ‘جام گلابست شاه’ (= ‘the king’s cup of rose‑water’ / ‘the king like a cup of rosewater’). Here کردار = ‘conduct/character’ and جام گلابست = ‘a cup of rose‑water’ (rose‑scented beverage) describing the king.
segments.s657-g874: که introduces a relative clause modifying the preceding image (‘which…’). از گرد is a prepositional phrase ‘from/by (a) dust’ (گرد = dust), یکباره is an adverb ‘at once/suddenly’, and گردد (3rd‑singular classical form of گردیدن/گشتن) here means ‘becomes’ (i.e. ‘becomes ruined’). تباه is the predicate adjective ‘ruined/corrupt’. The clause says the cup (image) is spoiled instantly by dust.
segments.s657-g875: ز (poetic) = ‘from’; the phrase 'ز دانا خردمند' = ‘from the wise and prudent’. بشنید is the simple past 3rd‑person singular ‘he heard’; پند is the object ‘advice/counsel’. Poetic inversion places the ablative phrase before the verb and object.
segments.s657-g876: خروشی (a roar/clamour) is the subject/noun; ز لشکر = ‘from the army’ (ablative); برآمد is past tense ‘arose/issued forth’; بلند qualifies the roar as ‘loud’. The order is noun + source + verb + adjective, a common poetic arrangement meaning ‘a loud cry rose from the army.’
segments.s657-g877: که introduces a clause (‘that’). آن لشکر اکنون جدا نیست = ‘that army is not now separate’; جدا نیست is the negative copula ‘is not separate’. زین here is a poetic noun (‘saddle/horse‑trappings’), so the phrase says the army is inseparable from its gear/horsemen—i.e. still together/mounted. Word order is slightly inverted for verse.
segments.s657-g878: همه آفرین باد is an optative formula ‘may all praise (be)…’ where آفرین is a noun ‘praise/acclamation’ and باد is the optative particle ‘may it be’. بر آن و این = ‘upon that and this’ (directing praise to both). The whole is a wish/blessing following the preceding statement.
segments.s657-g879: همه پاک = ‘all (of you) pure / keep everything pure’. در زینهار = ‘within/in (this) warning’ (زینهار = ‘warning/admonition’). منید is an archaic poetic exhortative form addressed to the addressees (2nd‑person plural imperative/subjunctive sense ‘keep/hold’); here the verb urges them to heed the warning. The phrase functions as an admonition.
segments.s657-g880: وزین = ‘and with this / by this (weighty, grave)’, where وزین = ‘weighty/dignified’; بر منش = ‘upon (your) character/nature’ (بر = on/upon, منش = character). یادگار منید repeats the same exhortative verb form ‘let it be a memorial’ or ‘make it a remembrance’ (یادگار = memento/memorial). The clause instructs them to take the matter as a solemn memorial upon their character.
segments.s657-g241: نگفتست is a past-form verb (poetic/older Persian) meaning literally 'did not say' here used with جز to mean 'said nothing except...'. Order is subject (مادر) after the verb, typical of Persian poetry. سخن (speech/words) is the direct object, and جز به داد restricts that object: 'except concerning justice.'
segments.s657-g242: This clause addresses the addressee: تو را دل is a topical/possessive pattern ('as for you — the heart', i.e. 'your heart') where را marks the topic/emphasis. چرا شد is a past-tense/resultative 'why became', with شد functioning as 'became'. ز + بیداد gives the cause 'from/because of injustice', and شاد is the predicate adjective 'glad/joyful.'
segments.s657-g243: ز لشکر is a prepositional phrase 'from the host/army'. بخوانیم is first-person-plural optative/subjunctive 'let us call/summon' (a hortative use common in epic narrative). چندی modifies مهان and here means 'some/a few', so the verb precedes its object (poetic word order).
segments.s657-g244: A string of modifiers describing those guests: خردمند 'wise' (adjective), و linking to برگشته 'returned' (past participle) which combines with گرد جهان 'around the world' (گرد as an old prepositional/adverbial element meaning 'around'). Together they form an attributive phrase 'wise and returned from around the world.'
segments.s657-g245: ز فرزانگان is 'from the sages' (agent/source). چون here functions as a subordinating conjunction ('as/when'), introducing a temporal/conditional clause. سخن is the object 'speech', and بشنویم is first-person-plural present/subjunctive 'we hear' or 'we shall hear' — it sets the condition for the main clause.
segments.s657-g246: برای و به گفتارشان بگرویم: برای here carries a sense like 'for/on account of' or simply 'with respect to', و links clauses, به introduces the target 'گفتارشان' (their speech), and بگرویم is a present-subjunctive/optative 'we will adopt/accept/follow' (i.e. accept their counsel). The whole means 'we will be for/adhere to their words.'
segments.s657-g247: ز ایوان مادر is 'from the mother's iwan/portico'. بدین گفت‌وگوی is a fused prepositional phrase meaning 'with this conversation' or 'after this talk' (بدین = بِ + این 'by/with this' in archaic/poetic form). The segment indicates place/source plus the circumstance of their departure.
segments.s657-g248: برفتند is simple past 'they went / departed'. و links the clauses. دلشان 'their heart' (دل with the third-person possessive suffix) is the subject of the following predicate. پر از جست‌وجوی is an adjectival phrase 'full of search/quest' (پر 'full', از marking the source/content, جست‌وجوی 'seeking').
segments.s657-g281: همه پادشاهی = 'the whole/entire kingdom' where همه (modifier) precedes the noun پادشاهی. شود is the third-person singular of شدن used here in the sense 'becomes / will be', and بر دو نیم is a prepositional phrase ('into/over two halves') with بر marking the division and دو نیم = 'two halves'. Word order: subject phrase + verb + prepositional complement.
segments.s657-g282: خردمند ماند = 'the wise remain / are left' where خردمند (a nominalized adjective) functions as 'the wise (person/people)' and ماند (ماندن) is the verb 'remain/be left'. به رنج وبه بیم is an adverbial phrase introduced by به twice (to/for/in), listing the states endured: رنج 'hardship' and بیم 'fear'.
segments.s657-g283: یکی ز انجمن = 'one (person) from the assembly'; یکی is an indefinite 'one/someone' and ز is the poetic preposition 'from'. سر برآورد is a verbal phrase 'raised (his) head' with سر as the object and برآورد (برآوردن) the verbal form 'lifted/raised'. راست follows to qualify the action as 'straight/upright.'
segments.s657-g284: به آوا سخن گفت = 'he spoke with a voice / aloud': به + آوا indicates manner ('with a sound/voice'), سخن is 'speech' (object/nominal element) and گفت is 'said/spoke' (verb). و برپای خاست = 'and rose to his feet', where برپای ('on foot/standing') + خاست (خاستن) 'arose' expresses standing up. The clauses are coordinated by و.
segments.s657-g285: که ما از دو دستور دو شهریار = a subordinate clause introduced by که ('that/regarding') with ما 'we' as subject. از here means 'concerning/from' and links to دو دستور ('the two orders/policies'); دو شهریار ('two kings') follows to specify whose orders these are. The phrase reads as 'that we, about the two plans/orders of the two kings...'.
segments.s657-g286: چه یاریم گفتن که آید به کار = 'what help is our speaking that it should come to use': چه 'what', یاریم functions as 'help/aid' here (a nominal notion of usefulness), گفتن is the infinitive 'speaking/to say' used nominally, and که آید به کار 'that it should come to use' (آید = 'come' used idiomatically 'prove useful'; به کار = 'to purpose/use').
segments.s657-g287: بسازیم فردا یکی انجمن = بسازیم is first-person plural hortative/subjunctive 'let us make/set up'; فردا 'tomorrow' indicates time; یکی انجمن 'one assembly' ('one' as a numeral modifying انجمن). The sequence is verb (hortative) + time adverb + object phrase.
segments.s657-g288: بگوییم با یکدگر تن به تن = بگوییم is first-person plural hortative 'let us speak'; با یکدگر 'with one another' (reciprocal); تن به تن is an idiom 'person to person' / 'face to face', literally 'body to body' formed by repeating تن with به between to mark the 'to' relation.
segments.s657-g265: This is a nominal phrase describing two people and their place: دلاور (an attributive adjective 'brave') + دو فرزانه ('two sages' — numeral before noun) followed by بردست راست, a locative phrase meaning 'at the right hand/side' (بردست used adverbially). Word order is adjective + numeral + noun, then location.
segments.s657-g266: همی here is an archaic/emphatic particle often prefacing actions; هریکی = 'each one' is the subject; ازجهان is a prepositional phrase 'from the world' (i.e. leaving life); بهرخواست functions as a past/actional verbal phrase 'departed/went away by his own will' (an older compound/poetic form meaning 'voluntarily'). The clause thus says each one left the world of his own accord.
segments.s657-g267: گرانمایگان را is the definite object marked by را ('the illustrious ones'); همه emphasizes 'all/everyone'; خواندند is a past plural verb here meaning 'they called/summoned' (classical usage where خواندن can mean 'to call/summon'). Word order: object + verb.
segments.s657-g268: بایوان is an older/contracted locative (here effectively 'to the portico' / 'in the hall'); چپ و راست = 'left and right' indicating the sides; بنشاندند is the past plural verb 'they seated (them)'. Together the phrase says they seated those called on the left and right of the portico.
segments.s657-g269: زبان ('speech/utterance') is the object in the idiom 'زبان برگشادند' meaning 'they opened their tongues' i.e. 'they began to speak'; برگشادند is the past plural 'they opened/revealed'; فرزانگان is the agent 'the sages'.
segments.s657-g270: که‌ای here introduces the direct address/vocative (classical 'که ای' = 'O ...'); سرفرازان ومردانگان are the vocatives 'exalted/honored ones and men' addressed by the sages. The phrase is the opening of a spoken address.
segments.s657-g271: ازین is the demonstrative 'of/from these' (از + این); نامداران 'the renowned/illustrious ones' qualifies them; فرخ‌نژاد is an epithet 'well-born/fortunate-born' attached to the noun phrase. Together: 'of these famed, well-born men'.
segments.s657-g272: که introduces a subordinate/relative clause 'that/which'; دارید is present second-person plural 'you have/hold' (here 'you observe/keep'); رسم پدرشان is the object 'the custom/rite of their father' with the possessive شان 'their'; به یاد is a fixed prepositional phrase 'in remembrance / in mind' meaning 'remember/keep in mind'. The clause asks/wonders whether you keep the father's custom in memory.
segments.s657-g137: ازان پس is an adverbial phrase meaning “after that/thereupon.” خنیده is a past participle/poetic perfect of خواندن used here as ‘spoke/sang/said,’ placed before its subject (poetic word order). بزرگان شهر is the subject (the city’s great ones, i.e. elders); note the verb-subject inversion common in classical Persian poetry.
segments.s657-g138: هرآنکس که ... is a relative clause: هرآنکس ‘whoever/anyone’ + که introducing the clause; اودارد (او دارد) is a present-tense verb ‘he/she has’ (here: ‘who has’ or ‘who holds’). از رای بهر is a prepositional phrase ‘concerning/of opinion, for the matter’ that completes the idea — literally ‘has (something) of opinion for (the matter).’ Grammatically this clause specifies those who spoke in the previous segment.
segments.s657-g139: یکایک is an adverb ‘one by one’/‘each in turn.’ بگوییم is the first-person-plural verbal form ‘we will say/we shall speak’ (here used to report that they will state opinions); the provided lemma/senses list refers to a noun (conversation), so the verbal sense is not among the given options. با رهنمون is a prepositional phrase ‘with guidance/guidance in hand,’ modifying how they will speak.
segments.s657-g140: The clause begins with the negator نه + the predicative خوبست (‘it is good’) so together ‘it is not good.’ گرمی here means ‘ardor/heat/strong passion’ (not literal temperature) and is the thing judged bad. به کاراندرون is a locative/target phrase ‘in/for the inner affairs (the harem/inner household)’ — note that کاراندرون is a compounded poetic term for the domestic inner circle.
segments.s657-g141: کسی کو بجوید همی is a classical phrasing: کسی ‘anyone’ + کو (an archaic relative ‘who’) + بجوید (present tense ‘seeks’) with همی marking continuous/habitual aspect (‘continually/seeks always’). تاج وگاه are the direct objects: تاج ‘crown’ and گاه here means ‘rank/position’ (a poetic use). Word order places the relative clause immediately after کسی.
segments.s657-g142: خردباید is a fused phrase ‘(one) needs wisdom’ or ‘wisdom is required’ (خرد + باید). What follows — ورای وگنج وسپاه — is a coordinated list introduced by poetic conjunctions (the initial و is attached to the following nouns): و رای (‘and counsel’), و گنج (‘and treasure’), و سپاه (‘and army’). Grammatically these are the things required alongside wisdom. Note that poetic orthography often attaches و to the next word.
segments.s657-g143: چو introduces a temporal/conditional clause (‘when’ or ‘if’). بیدادگر is the subject ‘a tyrant’ (one given to injustice). پادشاهی کند is the verb phrase ‘rules as king / takes kingship’ (کند is the present/future-form auxiliary ‘does/makes’). Together: ‘When a tyrant becomes king / rules…’.
segments.s657-g144: جهان پر ز ... کند is a causative/result clause: جهان ‘the world’ + پر ز ‘full of’ + the causes (گرم و تباهی) + کند ‘he makes/does.’ گرم here carries the sense of fervor/violent heat (metaphorical), and وتباهی is ‘and ruin/destruction.’ The overall meaning: he makes the world full of fervor and ruin.
segments.s657-g977: ز and از are poetic prepositions meaning 'from'; here the line begins with two sources in sequence ('from the sea and from ...'). دریا is the noun 'sea/ocean'. کنده is an adjective/noun 'uprooted' or 'dug up' modifying the following obscure compound وزرمگاه (an archaic/poetic place-name or compound). Word order is source1 و source2 (both governed by the prepositions).
segments.s657-g978: بگوییم is first-person plural subjunctive/optative—'let us say/speak' or 'we will say'—used here as an exhortation to narrate. با is the preposition 'with' and مرد جوینده راه is a noun phrase 'the man (mard) who is a seeker (juyande) of the way/path (rah)'; the modifier juyande follows the head noun as is normal in Persian.
segments.s657-g979: سواران is the plural subject 'horsemen/riders'. بهر سو means 'to every direction/side' (بهر = toward, سو = side/direction). پراگند is 'scattered' (predicate/adverbial). گو here is an archaic/verbalive element (‑گو) functioning like a participial or verbalizing suffix ('...‑ing' / '...‑like') attached to describe how they moved.
segments.s657-g980: بجایی literally 'to a place' (ب + جایی); که introduces a relative clause 'where/that'. بد modifies موبدی as 'bad/evil' (an epithet). موبدی is 'mōbed' (a Zoroastrian priest), and پیشرو is 'vanguard' or 'one who goes before'—here the clause specifies the place to which the riders went ('to the place where the bad mōbed was at the vanguard').
segments.s657-g981: سراسر is an adverb/adverbial 'throughout, all over'. بدرگاه شاه is a compound 'in the presence/court of the king' (بدرگاه = at the court/presence + شاه = king). آمدند is simple past third‑person plural 'they came' — here a narrative statement of arrival.
segments.s657-g982: بدان is an archaic fused locative/ demonstrative ('به + آن' = 'to that'); نامور is an adjective 'renowned/famous' modifying بارگاه 'palace/royal hall'. آمدند repeats the past plural 'they came' for emphasis; the second half restates destination more explicitly ('to that famed palace').
segments.s657-g983: جهاندار is the title/subject 'the sovereign' or 'world‑holder'. بنشست is simple past 'sat down' (he sat). با is the preposition 'with' and موبدان (plural of موبد) names the group of priests accompanying him; the verb precedes the adpositional phrase in normal Persian word order.
segments.s657-g984: This is a string of appositive nouns/adjectival epithets: بزرگان 'the great ones', دانادل 'those of wise-heart' (a compound epithet), و 'and', بخردان 'the prudent/wise'. They function as coordinated complements to indicate who sat with the sovereign.
segments.s657-g849: برادرش is برادر + possessive suffix -ش 'his brother' (subject). گریان و پرِ درد گشت is a predicate with گشت 'became' in the past; گریان 'weeping' and پرِ درد 'full of pain/sorrow' are predicative adjectives (پر = 'full (of)') attached to درد. Word order is subject (with suffix) + predicate (verb after adjectives), a common poetic inversion.
segments.s657-g850: This is a noun phrase: خروشِ سواران 'the cry/uproar of the horsemen' (خروش head, سواران in genitive). بران is a demonstrative/prepositional particle meaning 'on/over that' and modifies the following noun phrase پهن دشت 'the broad plain'. Together: 'the uproar of the horsemen over that wide plain.'
segments.s657-g851: خروشان is an adverbial/participial form 'with roaring' or 'tumultuously'. بغلتید is past plural 'they embraced/seized/threw themselves upon'. در پیش‌گو indicates the place/target 'at/in front of the soothsayer' (پیش‌گو = soothsayer), so the clause means 'tumultuously they fell upon the soothsayer.'
segments.s657-g852: همی‌گفت is the iterative past 'kept saying' (همی- marks continuous/repeated action). زار‌ای here functions as a lamenting vocative/exclamation 'Alas!' or 'O woeful one'. جهان‌دار نو is a vocative phrase 'O new/world-ruler', with جهان‌دار 'world‑ruler' and نو 'new' (addressing the newly installed sovereign).
segments.s657-g853: ازان پس is a temporal phrase 'from that / after that' (ازان = 'from that', پس = 'after/then'). بیاراست is a past verb 'arranged/put together' (here 'composed' or 'prepared') taking پند 'advice/admonition' as its object; فرزانه is the agent 'the sage' (verb–subject–object order is a poetic inversion).
segments.s657-g854: بگو گفت is a narrative formula: بگو (literally 'say') combined with گفت ('said') introduces direct speech — effectively 'he said'. کای is the vocative 'O' (not the Kay title here), followed by شهریار 'king' and بلند 'lofty' (an epithet). The phrase marks the opening of the sage's address to the king.
segments.s657-g855: ازین is a fused prepositional demonstrative 'from this' (از + این), introducing the topic. زاری و سوگواری 'wailing and mourning' are the nouns being questioned. چه سود follows to form the interrogative 'what profit/what use (is there)?' — literal: 'From this wailing and mourning, what benefit?'
segments.s657-g856: چنین رفت is an idiom 'thus it went / so it passed' (چنین = thus, رفت = went/passed). و links the clauses. این بودنی 'this being/existence' is a nominalized present (بودنی = 'that which is' or 'being'), and کار بود 'was the affair' (بود past 'was'). The clause comments philosophically that such transience is simply the nature of affairs.
segments.s657-g665: در + noun + past-prefixed verb: در گنج بگشاد uses در as the locative preposition 'in/within' followed by گنج 'treasury' and the past form بگشاد (b- + گشاد) meaning 'opened'. The conjunction و links the second clause روزی بداد where روزی (here a noun 'sustenance/provision' in older usage) is the object and بداد is the past verb 'gave' (classical word order: object before verb).
segments.s657-g666: سپاهش شد آباد is a nominal clause where سپاه + possessive suffix ش (سپاهش) 'his army' is the subject and شد آباد 'became prosperous' is a compound predicate (شد = become + آباد = prosperous). The following و با کام وشاد links an adverbial phrase ‘with satisfied desire and joyful’ (با + کام = 'with satisfaction/desire'; وشاد = 'and joyful'), where و beginning وشاد is the coordinating conjunction attached to شاد.
segments.s657-g667: This is a relative clause construction: سزاوار خلعت (deserving of the robe) modifies the following indefinite phrase هر آنکس که دید 'whomever he saw'. هر آنکس که دید literally 'every/whichever person that he saw' — هر + آنکس + relative که + past verb دید, with the verb final as is normal in classical Persian.
segments.s657-g668: بیاراست او را is a transitive past clause: بیاراست (he adorned/arrayed) takes او را (him, with را marking the definite object). چنانچون is a simile/degree adverb 'just as' and sزید here functions as 'deserved'/'merited' — together meaning 'he adorned him as was fitting/what he deserved.' Word order: verb (بیاراست) can precede object for emphasis, and چنانچون + سزید qualifies the manner.
segments.s657-g669: به دینار چون لشکر آباد گشت is a temporal/causal clause: به دینار 'by/with dinars' (instrumental), چون here introduces a temporal 'when'/'since' clause, and لشکر آباد گشت is 'the army became prosperous' (لشکر subject, آباد predicate adjective, گشت = became). The verb گشت is a past intransitive meaning 'became'.
segments.s657-g670: دل جنگجوی از غم آزادگشت is a nominative clause with دل جنگجوی 'the warrior's heart' as subject and آزادگشت (past, passive/intransitive) 'was freed/was liberated' as predicate; از غم is a prepositional phrase 'from sorrow' marking what the heart was freed from. Word order places the genitive noun (جنگجوی) after دل.
segments.s657-g671: پیامی فرستاد is a simple past clause 'he sent a message' (پیامی as indefinite/direct object). نزدیک گو is an imperative-like sequence where نزدیک 'near/nearby' addresses or targets the addressee and گو is the verbal imperative 'say' (or an instruction 'tell'); together they introduce the content to be communicated. The short word order places the verb گو after the adverb/adjective, typical of classical commands.
segments.s657-g672: که‌ای is the vocative/exclamatory 'O you' (که + ای) introducing direct speech; تخت را is the object 'the throne' marked by را; چون here functions as an interrogative 'how'; بپالیز is the 2nd/imperative-form verb 'purify/cleanse' (b- prefix + پالیز), and خو (an archaic form of 'خوی/خود') means 'self'/'one's own' or 'habit/disposition' — here governing the reflexive sense 'purify the throne yourself' or 'how do you keep the throne pure'. The clause is direct speech embedded after گو/that-introducer.
segments.s657-g65: دوهفته برآمد به زاری بمرد is best read as a clause describing a period of mourning leading to death. دوهفته (two weeks) is an adverbial time expression placed before the verb; برآمد is a past tense verb meaning “arose/occurred” (here ‘there arose’). به زاری is an adverbial phrase (به + noun) indicating the manner/condition (“with/in lamentation”), and بمرد is a past/optative-form verb used here to report his death (‘he died’). The sequence links as: “For two weeks lamentation arose; he died.”
segments.s657-g66: This segment reads as two coordinated actions: برفت (he departed/left) and جهان دیگری را سپرد (he handed the world to another). برفت is a simple past intransitive verb “he went away/left”; جهان دیگری (‘another world’ / ‘the world to another’) is the direct object of سپرد, and را is the Persian definite/object marker marking جهان دیگری as the object of سپرد (‘entrusted/left to’). سپرد is a past transitive verb meaning ‘entrusted/consigned.’
segments.s657-g67: همه (all/everyone) begins the noun phrase; سندلی here means those holding seats/offices (literally ‘seat-holders’). زار و گریان شدند uses two adjectival/participial predicates joined by و: زار (lamenting/wretched) and گریان (weeping). شدند is the past plural auxiliary/verb meaning ‘they became/they were,’ so the whole means ‘all the seat-holders became wretched and weeping.’
segments.s657-g68: ز (a poetic variant of از) introduces cause/agent ‘from/because of’; درد دل is a compound ‘pain of heart’ meaning sorrow/grief. مای here is a short pronoun/possessive or proper-name element linked to that sorrow (contextually ‘for/over Mây’ or ‘of us’ depending on reading), and بریان شدند is a passive/experiential expression ‘were burned/scorched’ (figurative: consumed by grief). شدند is the past plural copula/auxiliary. So: ‘Their hearts were scorched from the pain of Mây/our sorrow.’
segments.s657-g69: نشستند is a past plural verb ‘they sat/they held a sitting’ (here: sat in mourning). یک ماه is a duration adverbial ‘for one month.’ باسوگ is a compound meaning ‘in mourning’ (با + سوگ), and شاه is the noun ‘the king’ indicating with whom/at whose court they sat. The phrase thus means ‘they sat for a month in mourning with the king.’
segments.s657-g70: سرماه is a temporal noun ‘the start/beginning of the month.’ یک سر here means ‘one leader/chief’ (سر = head/leader) and functions as the subject that follows the temporal phrase; بیامد is a past tense verb ‘came,’ and سپاه is the noun ‘army/host’ (the larger group related to that coming). The clause reads: ‘At the beginning of the month one leader came — (i.e. an army arrived).’
segments.s657-g71: همه (all/everyone) quantifies the following nouns; نامداران are ‘the famous/renowned men’ and وگردان شهر is best read as ‘and the city’s گردان (troops/divisions).’ The phrase enumerates the important men and military units of the city.
segments.s657-g72: هرآنکس که (whoever that) introduces a relative clause. او را خرد بود is an older Persian construction meaning literally ‘wisdom was to him’ → ‘he had wisdom’ (او را marks the person as the dative/possessed, خرد ‘wisdom’ is the thing possessed, بود is the past copula). بهر is a preposition ‘for/for the purpose of’ and signals the intended goal or use; the clause is therefore ‘whoever among them had wisdom for…’ (the sentence continues afterward).
segments.s657-g761: ببخشم is a first-person present/future-form (ب‑ prefix + verb) meaning “I bestow / I will give.” The object phrase همه شهرها (literally “all the cities”) follows the verb here for poetic word order; بر سپاه is a prepositional phrase “upon/to the army” marking the recipient.
segments.s657-g762: چو‑فرمان = چو فرمان “when/if (the) command”; م + را here are separated in the segmentation but form مرا (“to me,” object pronoun + object marker). گردد is a present/subjunctive-form of گردیدن meaning “becomes / falls to.” The clause expresses a conditional time: “when/if command becomes mine, and (I have) the crown and seat.”
segments.s657-g763: بپاسخ here is an adverbial/inchoative phrase meaning “in answer / in response.” همه مهتران پیش اوی = “all the elders/chiefs in his presence” (مهتران = great men/chiefs; اوی = archaic/pronoun “him/his” meaning “before him”). The order places the response marker first for emphasis.
segments.s657-g764: یک‌یک نهادند is the past-tense verbal phrase “they placed/laid one by one” (یکایک = one by one; نهادند = placed/put down). برخاک روی = “(their) faces upon the ground” (برخاک = onto the ground; روی = face), i.e. they prostrated themselves. Word order is verb-final in the clause overall, but poetic inversion is present.
segments.s657-g765: که introduces an explanatory/reason clause (“for / that / because”). ما نام جوییم is present-tense clause “we seek a name/renown” (نام = fame/reputation; جوییم = we seek/search). و تو شهریار links directly: “and you (are) the sovereign,” addressing the king.
segments.s657-g766: ببینی here is the present/subjunctive 2nd-person form of دیدن used narratively as “you behold / you see.” کنون = “now.” گردش روزگار is an idiomatic compound: گردش = turn/rotation, روزگار = fate/times — together “the turning of (the) times/fate,” i.e. vicissitudes of fortune.
segments.s657-g767: ز درگاه = “from the court/threshold” (ز = from). طلخند is a poetic noun meaning “bitter laugh / wry smile,” and برشد خروش = “a cry/roar rose up” (برشد = it rose; خروش = cry/uproar). The verb is perfective/past narrative “rose.”
segments.s657-g768: ز لشکر همه کشور = “from the army of the whole country/realm.” آمد بجوش is a compound where آمد (came) combines with بجوش (to seethe/boil) to describe a surging, seething sound or movement — “there came a seething tumult” or “the whole army boiled up.”
segments.s657-g329: Imperative/advisory clause: بتا is an archaic/literary particle functioning here as a prohibitive 'lest / do not' (introducing what should be avoided); روی (noun) + بر (preposition) + خیره (adjective/participle 'staring') forms the prepositional phrase 'to stare upon the face'; چیزی is the object 'anything'; مجوی is the negative present imperative/subjunctive 'do not seek' placed at the end as the verbal directive. Word order is typical Persian: prepositional phrase and object precede the verb.
segments.s657-g330: A subordinate clause of caution/purpose introduced by که + negative subjunctive: که ... نبینند = 'lest / so that ... not see'. فرزانگان (subject) is 'the sages/wise'; آن is a demonstrative 'that' referring back to 'روی' (the face); نبینند is negative present-subjunctive/optative 'may not see' (used here to express the feared outcome); روی (repeated) is the noun 'face'.
segments.s657-g331: Main clause with reported speech: شنیدی is past 'you heard'; که introduces the content clause 'that ...'; جمهور 'the public/assembly' is the subject; تا + زنده بود = 'as long as / while [they] were alive' where تا functions as a temporal conjunction and بود is the past copula placed at the end (typical SOV ordering).
segments.s657-g332: Complement clause giving the comparison: برادر 'brother' is topic/subject; ورا is the enclitic object/pronoun 'to him' (vora = از + او in older/persianized form, here 'for him'); چون 'like/as'; یکی بنده 'one servant' is the simile's focus; بود is the past copula 'was' placed at the end. The sequence shows the typical Persian order: subject/topic → indirect object pronoun → simile phrase → verb.
segments.s657-g333: Compound clause: بمرد (poetic/archaic form related to 'مرد' = 'die') introduces the death of the other; او = 'he'; و = 'and'; من 'I' is the subject of the following verb; ماندم is the past tense 'I remained/was left' placed before adjectival complements; خوار 'despised/lowly' and خرد 'small/diminished' are predicative adjectives describing the speaker's state. Note the verb-final tendency inside each clause.
segments.s657-g334: General statement about ability: یکی 'one/single' modifies خرد here meaning 'wisdom' (the noun خرد is the direct object); را is the definite/object marker highlighting the object; گاه 'at times/occasionally' sets the temporal nuance ('not always'); نتوان is negative potential 'cannot' (present/potential form used with infinitive); سپرد is the infinitive/perfective 'to entrust' placed at the end—together 'گاه نتوان سپرد' = 'one cannot at times entrust'.
segments.s657-g335: Nominal clause: جهان 'the world' is the topic; پر ز ... is the idiomatic 'full of ...' construction with پر (full) followed by ز (poetic 'from/of') introducing the contents: خوبی 'goodness' and بد 'badness/evil'. از رای اوی is a prepositional phrase 'from his counsel' where رای = 'counsel/plan' and اوی is the archaic/enclitic form meaning 'his'. Word order places the source ('from his counsel') after the items it affects.
segments.s657-g336: Negative/capacity clause: نیارست here is a poetic/archaic predicate meaning (contextually) 'it was not fitting / it was not possible' or otherwise marks incapacity (the provided sense list does not capture this usage); جستن is the infinitive 'to attempt/seek'; کسی 'anyone' is the subject; جای اوی 'his place' where جای = 'place/position' and اوی = 'his'. In normal modern Persian order the verb/predicate follows the object phrase; here the infinitive jostan serves as the action that could not be performed.
segments.s657-g89: ازان پس is an adverbial phrase meaning 'from that time on/after that' (ازان = 'from that', پس = 'after'); هم 'also' modifies آموزگارش. آموزگارش is 'his teacher' (آموزگار + enclitic -ش = 'his'), and باش is the 2nd/3rd‑person imperative 'be' placed at the end as Persian puts the verb (here an imperative) after its complements.
segments.s657-g90: A string of predicate complements (دلارام, دستور, رایش) is listed before the final imperative تو باش ('you be'); دلارام here functions as a common noun 'comforter/solace' (vocative/appositive), دستور and رایش are further roles ('instruction/command' and 'opinion/judgment'), and تو باش is the vocative + imperative 'you be (these things)'.
segments.s657-g91: به گفتار ایشان is a prepositional phrase 'by/according to their word' (به ... گفتار = 'by the saying of'); ایشان 'they/them' supplies the possessor, and زن نیک‌بخت is the noun phrase 'the fortunate woman' (زن = woman, نیک‌بخت = fortunate) which serves as the subject of the following verb phrase.
segments.s657-g92: بیفراخت and بیاراست are past‑tense narrative verbs (3rd person) with the intensive/poetic bi‑ prefix; here they describe the actions done to the woman and to the throne. تاج is 'crown' (direct object of بیفراخت) and تخت 'throne' (object of بیاراست); the two verbs are joined by و 'and'.
segments.s657-g93: A series of abstract nouns joined by و ('and') is offered as a list of virtues/benefits (فزونی = abundance/increase; خوبی = goodness; فرهنگ = learning/culture; وداد = affection/love). The list functions as a nominal phrase describing what was present or bestowed.
segments.s657-g94: همه پادشاهی is the subject 'the whole kingdom' (همه = all/entire, پادشاهی = realm/kingdom). بدو is the dative 'to him/for him', گشت is the past of گشتن used causatively/transformatively 'became' and shad 'happy' is the predicative adjective: 'the whole kingdom became happy for him.'
segments.s657-g95: دوموبد is a compound noun meaning 'two mobeds' (a small group/title) functioning as the object; گزین کرد is the past tense 'chose' (گزین = choose, کرد = did/made), and پاکیزه‌رای is an adjectival phrase 'pure-minded/pure in judgment' qualifying those chosen. The verb گزین کرد is the main past action.
segments.s657-g96: هنرمند here describes the chosen persons as 'skillful/skillful people' (an additional epithet). سپرده is the past participle/past form 'entrusted/placed' (object/predicate) and the phrase به پای literally 'to the foot(s) of' is an idiom meaning 'into their care' or 'at their feet' — thus 'and he entrusted the world (گیتی) to them.' The sequence shows the king's further action after choosing the mobeds.
segments.s657-g969: نشستند is past 3rd‑person plural 'they sat/assembled'; هر دو is the subject 'both (of them)'; بهم (به + هم) here means 'together'. رای زن forms part of a following noun phrase and should be read as 'the woman of (or with) opinion/counsel' — i.e., introducing the woman who has a (spoken) view. Word order is verb → subject → adjunct → descriptive noun phrase, a common poetic order.
segments.s657-g970: گو here is adjectival/'-speaking' (a postpositive attribute: 'the speaking woman'); و is the coordinating 'and'; مرد فرزانه is 'the man, the sage/wise one'; بی‌انجمن is an adjectival phrase 'without company / without council', describing the man as solitary or out of assembly. The whole segment completes the apposition begun in the previous segment.
segments.s657-g971: بدو is به + او 'to him/unto him' (indirect object). گفت is the verbal past 'he said' (note: the supplied lemma senses here do not match the verbal reading). فرزانه names the speaker 'the wise man'; کای is an archaic/vocative 'O', and نیکخوی is a vocative adjective 'good‑natured one'. The order 'to him said the wise man, O ...' is inverted for poetic effect.
segments.s657-g972: نگردد is the negative present/subjunctive (or jussive/future sense) of 'گردیدن' with ن‑ prefix: 'will not become / will not come to pass'. بما = به + ما 'for us' or 'to us'. راست is 'true/real' and here marks the fulfilment/truth of آرزوی; این آرزوی = 'this wish'. The clause therefore means 'the truth/fulfilment of this wish will not come to us.' The verb appears clause‑initial as is common in poetic Persian.
segments.s657-g973: ز هر سو = 'from every side/quarter' (ز = from, هر سو = every direction). بخوانیم is 1st‑person plural present/subjunctive of خواندن here with the sense 'we call/summon' (classical Persian uses the present-subjunctive for hortative/narrative shades). برنا = 'youths' and و پیر = 'and the old (men)'. The verb precedes its objects, a normal poetic order.
segments.s657-g974: کجا is the interrogative/adverb 'where'. نامداری = 'fame/renown'; بود is the past copula 'was' (placing the predicate before/after the subject as permitted in poetic syntax); تیزویر appears as a proper name/compound (here treated as the entity whose renown is meant). The clause asks where Tizvīr's renown existed.
segments.s657-g975: ز کشمیر = 'from Kashmir'. وز here is a fused poetic form meaning 'and from' (the supplied senses for وز do not capture this usage, so treat as a connective+preposition); دنبر is a lexical item 'rear/loin' but here likely a place/name or part of a poetic list; و مرغ و مای = 'and (from) bird and (from) wine' — a cataloguing list typical in epic poetic diction. The prepositions and conjunctions mark the origins/sources enumerated.
segments.s657-g976: وزان = و + از + آن or 'and from those' (i.e., 'and from them/there'); تیزویران is the plural form (proper name or class) 'Tizvīrs' or 'the Tizvīr people'; جوینده is a participial noun 'seeking/seeker' and رای here is 'counsel/opinion' — together 'seekers of counsel'. The phrase means that from those places/things came Tizvīrs who sought counsel.
segments.s657-g473: نباید is an impersonal negative modal (‘it ought not / it should not be’). کزین is a fused preposition + demonstrative ('from this'), and فرجام کار is a noun phrase 'the outcome/result of the affair' (فرجام = end/outcome; کار = affair). Together this segment introduces the subordinate clause whose verb (بازماند) comes later.
segments.s657-g474: به ما is an indirect-object phrase 'to/for us'. بازماند is the main verb of the subordinate clause (3rd‑singular form meaning 'remains / is left'); بد روزگار is a postpositive nominal phrase 'ill-fortune' or 'bad times' describing the state left to us. The clause as a whole means '...is not to remain to us as ill-fortune.'
segments.s657-g475: بدو is the prepositional pronoun 'to him / unto him' (marking the indirect object). گفت here functions as the verb 'said' (classical word‑order places the verb near its arguments), فرزانه is the subject/agent 'the wise man', and کای شهریار is a vocative phrase ('O king') addressing the addressee before the quoted admonition.
segments.s657-g476: نباید again gives an evaluative/prohibitive sense ('it is not fitting / you should not'). تو را uses تو with the object/topic marker را to single out 'you' as the one concerned, and پندآموزگار is a predicate noun 'a teacher of admonition / moral instructor'. Persian omits the copula here, so the structure means 'It is not fitting that you be a moral-teacher.'
segments.s657-g477: گر introduces a conditional ('if'). از من places the action toward 'me' ('ask of me'), همی is the archaic/progressive particle marking ongoing or immediate action, بازجویی is the verbal/nominal form 'ask/inquiry', and سخن is the object 'a word / a question' — together: 'If you ask me (a question)...'.
segments.s657-g478: به جنگ برادر is a prepositional phrase indicating the context 'in/against (the) fight with a brother' (به + noun phrase). درشتی is a noun/adjective 'roughness, harshness' and مکن is the 2nd‑person singular negative imperative 'do not do'. The command warns: 'Do not act harshly toward your brother in the fight.'
segments.s657-g479: فرستاده‌ای is an indefinite agent-noun 'a sent one / messenger' (the form literally 'one sent'), modified by the adjective تیز 'swift'. نزدیک اوی is a prepositional/adverbial phrase 'near him' where اوی is the old/pronominal clitic 'him'. Together: 'a swift messenger near him' or 'send a swift messenger to him.'
segments.s657-g480: سرافراز is an epithet 'honored / lofty / triumphant' describing the envoy. با دانش means 'with knowledge' (learned), و links the next quality, and نرم گوی is an adjectival/verbal phrase 'soft‑speaking / gentle-speaking' (گوی here is the archaic verbal stem related to 'say', used descriptively or as an admonition 'speak gently'). The whole segment characterizes the messenger as dignified, learned, and gentle-speaking.
segments.s657-g385: سپهر (subject) comes first, followed by the locative phrase اندرَان رزمگه (‘in/within that battlefield’), and the clause ends with the intransitive copular verb خیره شد — خیره is an adjectival/state word (“astonished/struck dumb”) and شد marks the change of state (“became/was”). The word order (Subject + locative + predicate) is typical of classical Persian poetry and اندرَان is an archaic/poetic locative meaning ‘in/therein’.
segments.s657-g386: ز introduces a cause/ablative phrase ‘from/because of’; گرد here means ‘dust/cloud’ and combines with سپه (‘the host/army’) to yield ‘the army’s dust’. چشمها is the grammatical subject and تیره شد is the predicate (‘became dark’), where تیره is an adjectival predicate and شد marks the change. The whole phrase expresses a result caused by the army’s dust.
segments.s657-g387: بر آمد is a compound verbal phrase meaning ‘rose up/came forth’; here it takes the masdar/infinitive خروشیدن (‘roaring’) as the notional noun: “a roaring arose.” گاو دم is a genitive/possessive grouping (the bull’s breath/expulsion) specifying the source of that roar, with دم as ‘breath’ or ‘blast’. The sequence is poetic and compact: Verb + verbal-noun + source.
segments.s657-g388: ز is the ablative/prepositional marker ‘from’; دو رویه آواز is literally ‘a two‑sided/dual‑facing voice’ (i.e., a sound coming from both sides). رویینه خم is a post‑nominal descriptive phrase: رویینه (‘armored, brazen/iron‑clad’) qualifies خم (‘curve/arched piece’), so the phrase describes the curved iron piece (likely a trunk or horn) whose sound is heard. Adjectival modifiers follow the noun in this construction.
segments.s657-g389: بیاراست is a past verb meaning ‘he adorned/arrayed’ (or ‘he was well‑adorned’ depending on context). با is the preposition ‘with/by’; میمنه is a noun meaning ‘the right side/right flank’ and functions as the object of با. میسره here follows and appears as an adverbial/qualifier (‘with ease/attainably’ in the given sense list), so the phrase reads ‘arrayed with the right (side) with ease’ — a compact poetic phrasing describing how he was arrayed.
segments.s657-g390: تو is the 2nd‑person subject ‘you’; گفتی is the past tense verb ‘you said’ (here introducing reported speech). The reported clause is زمین کوه شد یکسره: زمین (the earth/ground) + کوه شد (‘became a mountain’) + یکسره (‘completely/altogether’). The structure is Subject (تو) + verb (گفتی) + reported clause (that the earth wholly became a mountain).
segments.s657-g391: دولشکر is a fused numeral‑noun (‘two armies’) serving as the plural subject; کشیدند is the past plural verb ‘they drew up/drew’ (here ‘drew up’ a line); صف is the direct object ‘line/formation’; بر دو میل is a prepositional phrase ‘on/at two masts/standards’ (میل used in the sense ‘pillar/standard’). The verb agrees with the plural subject and the clause describes the deployment.
segments.s657-g392: دو شاه (‘two kings’) is the noun phrase subject, modified by the adjective سرافراز (‘honored/triumphant’) placed after it in poetic style; بر پشت پیل is a prepositional locative ‘upon the back of the elephant’ (پشت = back, پیل = elephant). The short clause states the kings’ lofty position atop the elephant.
segments.s657-g801: 'همه' qualifies 'دشت' as 'the whole/plain entire plain'; 'مغز و جگر' are predicative nouns linked to 'بود' (past copula) meaning 'the plain was (made up of) brains and livers' — a graphic description of entrails on the field. The final 'و دل' is coordinated with the previous clause and left elliptical here, preparing the contrast or continuation realized in the next segment.
segments.s657-g802: 'همه' here = 'all/every' modifying 'نعل اسبان' (a possessive/genitive phrase 'horseshoes of the horses'); Persian poetic word order sometimes omits the ezāfe. 'ز' is the poetic preposition 'with/from' marking contents; 'پر' is an adjectival/verb-like word meaning 'full (of)' and applies to 'نعل' with two complements 'ز خون' and 'ز گل' = 'full of blood and of mud'.
segments.s657-g803: 'نگه کرد' is a compound verbal expression: 'نگه' (a gaze/look) + past verb 'کرد' = 'he cast/gave a look' (here translated 'cast a smile/look'). 'طلخند' is the direct object of this action (a bitter/wry smile). 'از پشت پیل' is a prepositional phrase 'from behind the elephant' where the ezāfe between 'پشت' and 'پیل' is omitted in poetic diction.
segments.s657-g804: 'زمین دید' = 'he saw the land/earth' (noun + past verb 'دید'). 'برسان' is the jussive/imperative form of رساندن ('bring/deliver') and here functions as an order whose direct object (e.g. 'خبر' or 'this') is omitted — a common poetic compression. 'دریای نیل' is the destination 'to the sea of the Nile' placed after the verb.
segments.s657-g805: 'همه باد' = 'all the winds' (جمع generalization). 'بر سوی' is a two-word prepositional phrase 'toward' (بر + سوی) followed by its goal 'طلخند' (either the person or the grim smile). 'گشت' is the past/inchoative verb 'turned/became' (here 'turned toward').
segments.s657-g806: The repeated 'به' marks two directions/objects: 'به راه و به آب' = 'toward the road and toward the water'. 'آرزومند' is an adjectival predicate 'desirous/longing' and with the verb 'گشت' ('became') expresses a change of state: '(the winds) became desirous/longing (for road and water)'.
segments.s657-g807: The repeated preposition 'ز' ('from/because of') lists the adverse elements: 'باد' (wind), 'خورشید' (sun), and 'شمشیر تیز' (a sword qualified by the following adjective 'تیز' = 'sharp'). The adjective follows the noun in typical Persian order; the list sets up causes or sources of distress.
segments.s657-g808: The correlative negation 'نه ... و نه ...' means 'neither ... nor ...'. 'آرام دید' literally 'saw calm/peace' — here idiomatically 'there was no calm/peace' — and 'راه گریز' is a noun phrase 'a way of escape/flight'. The past verb 'دید' governs both negated coordinates; the construction conveys absence of rest and absence of escape.
segments.s657-g409: یکی گوش بگشای is an imperative phrase: یکی (a single/one) quantifies گوش (ear) and places the numeral before the noun, idiomatically 'lend an ear' or 'listen a bit'. بگشای is the present/imperative stem 'open' (بـ- prefix + گشای), and بر + پندگو places the target ('to the adviser') after the verb.
segments.s657-g410: به گفتار بدگوی غره مشو is a negative imperative: مشو ('do not become') governs the preceding prepositional phrase. به گفتار = 'by/through the speech' (به used in the sense 'by'), بدگوی functions as a noun/adjective 'the slanderer' or 'ill-speaker', and غره ('deluded') describes the state to avoid: 'do not be deceived.'
segments.s657-g411: نباید is an impersonal negative 'should not / must not'; که introduces the subordinate clause ('that ...'). از ما = 'from/of us' (marks source). بدین is built from ب- + این and here means 'in/at this' (with ب as prepositional prefix and 'این' 'this'); کارزار is the noun 'battle/field' where the reproach would occur.
segments.s657-g412: This is the clause's predicate. نکوهش is the noun 'reproach/disapproval' and بود is the copular verb 'was/would be' (here signalling existence/possibility). در جهان یادگار is a locative/comitative phrase 'as a memorial/in the world' modifying نکوهش بود (i.e. 'a reproach that remains as a memorial in the world').
segments.s657-g413: که introduces the result/feared clause 'that ...'. این کشور هند = 'this country, India' (demonstrative + noun + apposition). ویران is an adjective 'ruined/laid waste', and شود is the third-person subjunctive/present of شدن used here to mean 'become / be made' — together 'be laid waste'.
